data.text: Using the `ddx` workflow because this is an explicit bead execution. First I’m checking the bead’s scope against the repo state, then I’ll trace the decomposition and doctor paths that could be creating parent-as-dep edges.

docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md:181:Auto-recovery is a distinct stage that runs **between** Layer 3 (queue drain) and the `status=proposed` operator-review escape. It is commonly triggered when a bead's within-cycle escalation ladder has been exhausted on two or more consecutive drain cycles (`Extra["consecutive_ladder_exhaustions"] >= 2`; see TD-031 §5 (`consecutive_ladder_exhaustions` Policy) field). The counter is a coordination hint, not a brittle gate: DDx may derive the same condition from attempt/review events, and explicit too-large, needs-decomposition, child-depth-cap, or no-changes-decompose classifications may enter auto-recovery immediately per TD-031 §3.3. The bead must be `status=open` and not carry a valid operator-authored `recovery:manual` label.
docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md:190:2. **Decompose attempt** (only when reframe returned no change or the reframer invocation failed) — dispatch a strong-power decomposer agent with the same `MinPower` floor. The decomposer returns 2–5 executable child bead specs when the bead can be split under itself. If child depth is exhausted, it returns sibling or replacement bead specs under the nearest safe parent/root. DDx calls `Store.Create` for each generated bead, wires the required dependency or supersession edges, records `decompose-applied` (TD-027 §13), and leaves the oversized bead `status=open` with `Extra["execution-eligible"]=false` when it should no longer be claimed directly. The queue advances through the generated executable work.
docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md:195:The auto-recovery sequence runs within the layer-3 run record as an additional stop-condition evaluation entry. The entry names the bead, the trigger condition (`consecutive_ladder_exhaustions` value), which recovery step was attempted, the outcome, and the cost of any reframer/decomposer invocations. These costs contribute to the drain-level budget cap (FEAT-014).
docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md:199:- **TD-031 §2** — `reframe_applied`, `decompose_applied`, `auto_recovery_failed`, and `per_bead_budget_exhausted` outcome rows define the status transitions and Extra mutations.
docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md:202:- **TD-031 §4 (Auto-Recovery Role Catalogue)** — reframer and decomposer dispatch contracts.

Every install/lookup path is hardcoded to the per-project tree via ddxroot.Path(). Before `--global` install (sibling bead) or `ddx init --global` (sibling bead) can exist, there must be a single resolver that, given a plugin name, returns its on-disk path by checking the project layer first, then a global layer, then the baked-in default.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- cli/internal/registry/installer.go:66 `InstallPackageFromFS(pkg, src, projectRoot)` only knows the project root; there is no global target.\n- cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:75 `Path()` resolves only the project root (in-tree `\u003cproject\u003e/.ddx` or convention `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/...` at line 86). There is no `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global` helper.\n- cli/internal/skills/install.go:28 `Install(src, projectRoot, opts)` and all callers assume a single project destination.\n- No `ResolvePlugin`-style function exists anywhere under cli/internal/registry.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n1. Add `GlobalDir()` to cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go returning `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global` (reuse the existing `xdgDataHome()` helper at ddxroot.go:89). The plugins root is `GlobalDir()/plugins`.\n2. Add `registry.ResolvePlugin(ctx, projectRoot, name string) (path string, layer string, error)` implementing precedence:\n   - `ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot)/plugins/\u003cname\u003e` if the dir exists → layer \"project\".\n   - `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/\u003cname\u003e` if it exists → layer \"global\".\n   - baked-in default (defaultplugin.FS, the `ddx` plugin only) → layer \"baked-in\"; return an error for any other unresolved name.\n3. Export the layer string so doctor/init/install (sibling beads) can report which layer answered.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Wiring `--global` into install/update commands (sibling bead: install/update --global).\n- `ddx init --global` and agent-tier symlinks (sibling bead: init topology).\n- `ddx doctor` reporting (sibling bead: doctor + specs).\n- Removing the existing per-project install. Both layers coexist.\n\nDEPS\nddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — `ddxroot.Path()` and the new `GlobalDir()` must share the same XDG_DATA_HOME convention agreed there. Sibling beads in this set depend on this resolver.","events_attachment":"ddx-04566a8f/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-04566a8f","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:config","area:plugin","area:cli","kind:feature","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-20de9a81","status":"closed","title":"registry: add two-layer plugin resolution (project → global → baked-in) with global XDG path helper","updated_at":"2026-06-02T01:56:37.269486624Z"}

It does not look for open beads X with superseded-by==Y and cascade-close them. FEAT-010 §Idle-Path Diagnosis and Auto-Remediation now requires this cascade with a strict scope guard.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nExtend cli/internal/bead/store.go Close(Y) with a post-close cascade step. After Y closes, scan for open beads X where supersession-by-Y is the sole remaining reason to stay open. Strict scope contract (each must hold; if any fails, surface in ddx work focus Section A, do NOT cascade):\n- X is open AND (X.superseded-by == Y OR X carries label superseded-by:Y) AND Y is now closed.\n- X has no other open dependents (no open bead Z with Z.dependencies referencing X).\n- X has no open dependencies of its own beyond the supersession marker.\n- X has attempt_count == 0.\n- X has no operator notes added after the supersession marker timestamp.\n- X is not currently claimed or in-progress (no live claim_id).\n- X carries no manual-hold / no-auto-close / container override label.\n\nRe-read X's current tracker state inside the same Close(Y) invocation before mutating — a concurrent worker may have changed X since the scan started.\n\nIdempotent: re-running Close(Y) is a no-op for already-closed X.\nCycle-guarded: track visited IDs within one Close(Y) call; cascade is one hop deep. If X is a superseder for an open Z, Z's cascade is DEFERRED to a separate operator action (do not transitively cascade).\nAudit: emit a superseded_close event on X with body {closed_by_cascade_of: Y, reason: \"closed_as_superseded_via:Y\"}. Operators can grep events to reverse.\n\nAdd operator override flag at the work command surface in a separate bead (--no-auto-supersede-close); the Close() code path always runs the cascade unless the flag has been threaded through. For this bead, accept the cascade always firing; the flag wiring is the work-loop bead's responsibility.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Cascading transitively beyond one hop (deferred to a future bead if ever needed).\n- The work-loop wiring of --no-auto-supersede-close flag (separate bead).\n- RC-3 dead-intermediate cleanup walk-up (separate bead, depends on this one).\n\nPARENT AND DEPS\nParent: ddx-2a1ece2b. All DDx state for that project (beads.jsonl, attachments, archive, plugins, etc.) lives there and is versioned. Two concurrent ddx invocations on the same project must not race-create or race-write worktrees.json. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.\n\nObserved scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):\n- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')\n- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)\n- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]\n- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores\n- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order\n\nRoot cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. TestConventionMode_ReadsGlobalSkillsBeforeHardcoded asserts that with --global installed, convention mode picks up the global plugin instead of the hardcoded one.\n6. ddx doctor's \"Global Install\" check exists and reports the install path + status.\n7. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/... ./internal/config/... green.\n8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-05-15T01:09:39.694724565Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-15c71970","depends_on_id":"ddx-564ea1cf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-15T01:10:33Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-15c71970","depends_on_id":"ddx-ad338ec3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-15T01:10:34Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM\nConvention mode (per ddx-ad338ec3) bootstraps an XDG project dir but has no source for skills, personas, or the default ddx plugin. Either every project re-downloads the plugin on first use, or the user must have a global install. The latter is the better answer: install once per machine, all convention-mode projects share.\n\nROOT CAUSE\nThere is no per-user install path today. `ddx init` only writes per-project state. Skills/personas live in the project's .ddx/plugins/ (in-tree) or are missing entirely (convention).\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n\n1. Add `ddx init --global` flag (and corresponding code path in cli/cmd/init.go). When set:\n   - Resolve target path: ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/ (defaults to ~/.local/share/ddx/global/).\n   - Install the default ddx plugin (library/ contents) into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/.\n   - Write a default ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults (library URL, branch, etc.).\n   - Idempotent: re-running upgrades to a newer plugin version but does not destroy operator state.\n\n2. Convention mode reads ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/ as the second precedence tier (in-tree \u003e global \u003e hardcoded). If --global has never been run, fall back to baked-in hardcoded defaults — convention mode still works without it; --global is an optimization for users who want offline-cacheable plugin content.\n\n3. Document the install path in ddx doctor: a new check \"Global Install\" reports whether ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/ exists and points operators to `ddx init --global` if it does not.\n\n4. Do NOT remove the existing per-project plugin install (in-tree mode at .ddx/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/). Both coexist.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- In-project ddx init manifest write (E4 bead).\n- Migration from legacy ~/.config/ddx/ or ~/.ddx/ if such exist on disk (none expected; today there is no global install).\n- Plugin update / upgrade flow for the global install (use existing `ddx update` mechanism; verify it works against the new path).\n\nDEPS\nddx-564ea1cf (E1) — resolver must exist.\nddx-ad338ec3 (E2) — convention mode reads from this path.","id":"ddx-15c71970","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:config","area:cli","kind:feature","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint"],"notes":"Superseded by epic restructure into 3 consolidated beads aligned to the two principles: (1) bulletproof ddx home resolution, (2) centralized vs in-project skills/personas. New beads pending.","parent":"ddx-602ae31a","priority":2,"schema_version":1,"status":"cancelled","title":"config: ddx init --global installs user-tier skills/personas to XDG global","updated_at":"2026-05-15T01:16:05.901133882Z"}

GlobalPluginsDir()/\u003cname\u003e if it exists -\u003e scope 'global'.\n   c. baked-in default plugin (only name=='ddx', via internal/registry/defaultplugin) -\u003e scope 'baked-in'.\n   d. otherwise ok=false.\n3. .ddx/beads.jsonl:87:{"acceptance":"1. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestExecuteBead.*Cleanup|TestCleanupAttemptWorktree' -count=1` passes.\n2. A unit test exists for each of these non-success outcomes asserting WorktreeRemove was called: provider_connectivity, no_evidence_produced, execution_failed, structural_validation_failed, land_conflict, post_run_check_failed.\n3. A unit test asserts the success-path classifier does NOT call WorktreeRemove from the new hook (landing path retains ownership).\n4. A unit test asserts `ddx try --no-merge` preserves the worktree regardless of outcome.\n5. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./... -count=1` passes.\n6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","closing_commit_sha":"e979bcd786ff98bd4767596dd1ed2dbe76cddb0c","created_at":"2026-05-29T02:02:14.061932044Z","description":"PROBLEM: When a `ddx try`/`ddx work` attempt fails with any non-success\noutcome (`provider_connectivity`, `no_evidence_produced`, `execution_failed`,\n`structural_validation_failed`, `land_conflict`, `post_run_check_failed`),\nits execute-bead worktree under `~/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-\u003cbead\u003e-\u003cattemptID\u003e/`\nis not removed. Cleanup happens only when a *subsequent attempt of the same bead*\nis launched and `RecoverOrphans` runs as pre-spawn sweep.\n\nOBSERVED 2026-05-28 in niflheim drain: a 6.6 GB worktree\n`.execute-bead-wt-niflheim-07f6f8ff-20260528T164209-f179349f` remained 9+\nhours after its attempt failed with `provider_connectivity` (bragi 502).\nThe bead's retry resolved as `already_satisfied` without spawning a new\nexecute-bead worktree (the AC was already met by partially-landed commits\nfrom the failed attempt), so `RecoverOrphans` was never called for that\nbead — the leak is permanent until another attempt at the same bead runs.\n\nThe user reports seeing this pattern consistently across drains, not just\non the one observed bead.\n\nROOT CAUSE: `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:393-411`\ndefines `RecoverOrphans` with the docstring \"the parent's responsibility —\ncall it before spawning new workers so stale worktrees from crashed previous\nattempts do not accumulate.\" The function matches strictly by\n`basenamePrefix := ExecuteBeadWtPrefix + beadID + \"-\"` and is only invoked\non a new-attempt pre-spawn path. Two leak conditions follow:\n\n  (a) Bead's retry succeeds via `already_satisfied` (no new worktree\n      spawned because the AC check resolves before agent invocation).\n      The orphan from the prior failed attempt is never matched.\n  (b) Bead is closed for any reason after a failed attempt and is never\n      re-attempted (most common in real flows — failures often resolve\n      via decomposition into different child beads, never returning to\n      the original).\n\nThe post-attempt outcome classifier at\n`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1966` (Classify worker outcome:\ntask_succeeded / task_failed / task_no_changes / ...) is the natural place\nto call `WorktreeRemove` for non-success classes, but does not.\nThe existing landing path at\n`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1088,1329,1679` calls\n`gitOps.RemoveWorktree` correctly for landing-time temp worktrees, but\nthose are different (per-landing) worktrees, not the per-attempt execute-bead\nworktree under `~/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/`.\n\nPROPOSED FIX: Add an attempt-finalize cleanup hook that runs after outcome\nclassification (around `execute_bead.go:1966-2017`) and removes the\nper-attempt execute-bead worktree when the outcome is anything other than\n`task_succeeded`. Preserve only when:\n  1. The operator explicitly opted in via `ddx try --no-merge` (preserve\n     for inspection regardless of outcome).\n  2. An `executions_mirror.go` write or evidence-capture step requires the\n     worktree to persist past classification (verify by reading the call\n     chain at `execute_bead.go:2069-2114`).\n\nImplementation steps the executing agent should follow:\n  1. Read `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1423-2017` to confirm the\n     outcome-classification flow and identify the right post-classify hook\n     point.\n  2. Add a helper `cleanupAttemptWorktree(gitOps, workDir, wtPath, outcome,\n     preserveFlag)` that calls `gitOps.WorktreeRemove(workDir, wtPath)` for\n     non-success outcomes unless `preserveFlag` is set.\n  3. Wire it into the outcome-classification path.\n  4. Add a unit test asserting the cleanup branch for each non-success\n     outcome class, and a negative test confirming success-path keeps the\n     worktree (landing path owns that cleanup).\n  5. Update `RecoverOrphans` docstring to reflect that it's now a\n     belt-and-suspenders sweep, not the primary cleanup path.\n\nNON-SCOPE:\n- Changing `RecoverOrphans` behavior or call sites (keep it; it covers\n  hard-crash cases the new hook can't reach).\n- Adding a periodic background sweeper or `ddx doctor` flag for stale\n  worktrees (potential follow-up).\n- Changing the worktree path under `~/.cache/ddx/exec-wt/`.\n- Changing outcome-classification logic or outcome taxonomy.\n- Adding telemetry/metrics for leak rates.\n\nIN-SCOPE FILES:\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go (add the cleanup hook + helper)\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_test.go or sibling _test file (add\n  cleanup tests for each non-success outcome)\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go (docstring update on\n  RecoverOrphans only; behavior unchanged)\n\nOUT-OF-SCOPE FILES:\n- execute_bead_land.go — landing-time temp-worktree cleanup is correct\n  and untouched.\n- Any code under cli/cmd/ — no CLI surface change required.\n- docs/ — documentation update is downstream of the spec-evolution bead\n  ddx-828a1b8f (worktree resource isolation) and not required here.","events_attachment":"ddx-274943fc/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-274943fc","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:execution","kind:bug","kind:operability","phase:iterate","spec:FEAT-010"],"needs-human-reason":"consecutive_wedge","needs-human-since":"2026-05-31T05:20:40Z","needs-human-source":"ddx work","needs-human-suggested-action":"investigate the repeated wedge (resolver hang / phase-empty heartbeats) and clear the operator-attention state once resolved","needs-human-summary":"bead wedged on 2 consecutive claims (\u003e= threshold 2; last wedge \"progress_watchdog\"); stopped re-claiming and flagged for operator attention","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-90ab9f5e","spec-id":"FEAT-010","status":"closed","title":"execute-bead: clean up worktree on non-success attempt outcomes (currently leaks on failed-then-retried-as-already_satisfied path)","updated_at":"2026-06-06T02:28:44.767198655Z"}

The new work-loop contract requires one vocabulary across CLI parsing, worker persistence, REST, and GraphQL: mode plus idle_interval, with no public compatibility alias for the removed polling flag.\n\nROOT CAUSE\nEach work field traverses six independent declaration points with no compile-time linkage:\n\n1. cobra flag extraction plus local vars: cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1582-1605 extracts fromRev, harness, model, profile, provider, modelRef, effort, once/watch mode, idleInterval, noReview, reviewHarness, reviewModel, maxCostUSD, requestTimeout, minPower, and maxPower as naked locals.\n\n2. positional-param pass-down: cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1744-1773 closes over these locals and assembles config.CLIOverrides by hand; no single struct groups them.\n\n3. ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec definition: cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49 is hand-maintained in parallel with cobra and has historically missed fields.\n\n4. handleStartExecuteLoopWorker request struct: cli/internal/server/server.go:2395-2409 is a separate anonymous struct, so newly added fields can be silently lost for server-spawned workers.\n\n5. StartExecuteLoop literal: cli/internal/server/server.go:2441-2455 plus cli/internal/server/workers.go:345-361 hand-assembles the worker spec; any field absent in step 4 cannot appear here.\n\n6. GraphQL adapter request struct plus StartExecuteLoop literal: cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:63-76 plus 127-141 is a third parallel declaration; fixes to REST can leave GraphQL broken.\n\nThe consumer (cli/internal/server/workers.go:658-926 runWorker) reads MaxCostUSD from escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD hardcoded (line ~753) instead of spec.MaxCostUSD because the field was never plumbed through. OpaquePassthrough has two validation skip gates that must both fire (workers.go:357-361 and the ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService branch in runWorker).\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- cli/internal/agent/spec.go or cli/internal/agent/executeloop/spec.go: define ExecuteLoopSpec with all fields currently spread across the six layers. Fields: ProjectRoot, Harness, Model, Profile, Provider, ModelRef, Effort, LabelFilter, Mode, IdleInterval, NoReview, ReviewHarness, ReviewModel, OpaquePassthrough, MaxCostUSD, RequestTimeout, MinPower, MaxPower, FromRev, SpecVersion int. JSON tags drive both REST wire format and disk persistence.\n- Mode is the canonical once/drain/watch selector. CLI --once maps to Mode=once. CLI --watch maps to Mode=watch. Default is Mode=drain.\n- IdleInterval is meaningful only for Mode=watch. --idle-interval without --watch is a usage error in the cobra layer and must not be represented as a separate polling mode in the persisted spec.\n- Do not add, accept, document, or emit a public compatibility alias for the removed polling flag. Historical worker records may be read best-effort as unknown/legacy input, but new CLI/server/API inputs use Mode plus IdleInterval.\n- Add Spec.Validate() and Spec.ApplyDefaults(). Remove positional-param closure pattern; pass spec by value.\n- cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49: replace ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec with (or alias to) ExecuteLoopSpec from the new package. Call ApplyDefaults() then Validate() before StartExecuteLoop.\n- cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:63-76 plus 127-141: delete the parallel anonymous struct and parallel StartExecuteLoop literal; route through the same server-side ApplyDefaults + Validate + StartExecuteLoop as REST.\n- cli/internal/server/workers.go:~753: replace hardcoded escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD with spec.MaxCostUSD, with DefaultMaxCostUSD as fallback when zero.\n- cli/internal/agent/spec_test.go or cli/internal/agent/executeloop/spec_test.go: TestExecuteLoopSpec_RoundTripsAllFields_Reflection uses reflect over exported fields; sets non-zero value per field; marshals; unmarshals; asserts round-trip. agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=8503 cost_usd=0.8610"},{"actor":"erik","body":"Architectural conflict with open bead ddx-eccc6efb (ADR-022 step 7), plus the bead materially exceeds the size-check thresholds. Human triage required to resolve the design conflict before any unification work commits. ## Summary The bead asks to UNIFY the parallel ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec / cobra-flag / HTTP-request structs into one canonical `ExecuteLoopSpec` struct used at every layer. But the open ADR-022 step 7 epic (ddx-eccc6efb, status=open) takes the opposite direction: DELETE `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` entirely and replace the in-process server-spawn path with `exec ddx work` (subprocess), at which point there are no parallel structs because the server stops marshalling a spec at all — it just builds an argv vector. These are mutually-exclusive architectural directions for the same code. Landing this bead's unification would either: (a) be partially undone when ADR-022 step 7 deletes the unified struct, or (b) lock in the unified-struct design and require ADR-022 step 7 to be re-scoped or abandoned. The bead's \"SEQUENCING WITH IN-FLIGHT BEADS\" section names dc157075, 9d55601f, and a946c744, but is silent on ddx-eccc6efb / ADR-022 step 7. That gap is the load-bearing decision this bead does not make. ## Evidence 1. `cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49` — current `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` exists with the fields the bead lists (and is missing MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower/FromRev as the bead claims). 2. ADR-022 step 7 (ddx-eccc6efb) is OPEN with acceptance: \"Legacy ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec serialization deletes when this lands.\" Confirmed via `ddx bead show ddx-eccc6efb`. 3. ADR-022 step 4 (ddx-85165814) already CLOSED → `--local` is a deprecated no-op (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1576-1577`). The cobra path no longer branches to server-submit; `executeLoopWithServer` (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1986`) is unreferenced (verified: only the function definition matches; no call sites). 4. Therefore the bead's USER-OBSERVED SYMPTOM (\"ddx work --harness claude works under --local but fails when going through the server\") cannot be reproduced via the CLI today — `--local` is a no-op and the server-submit branch from cobra is dead code. The remaining server-spawn path is via `POST /api/agent/workers/work` (`cli/internal/server/server.go:2390`) and the GraphQL `workerDispatchAdapter` (`cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:127`), both of which ADR-022 step 7 wants to delete in favor of subprocess exec. 5. Recent ddx-eccc6efb attempt (20260503T065047-61e4ed20, result.json) was DECLINED for size and decomposed into proposed children 7a/7b/7c — those children may not yet be filed in the main repo per its own bookkeeping note, but the intent stands. ## Size-check verdict (independent of the conflict) The bead also fails the size check on its own: - 17 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems (cobra flag binding, client-side validation, HTTP handler, GraphQL adapter, workers.go consumer, on-disk persistence, backward-compat for legacy fields, forward-compat with SpecVersion, custom Duration codec, reflection-driven test, manual end-to-end run). - Touches at minimum: new package `cli/internal/agent/executeloop/` (struct + Validate + ApplyDefaults + custom Duration + tests), `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go` (cobra rebind + dispatch split), `cli/internal/server/server.go` (handler rewrite + handler tests), `cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go` + `graphql_adapters_default_profile_test.go` + `workers_config_test.go` (parallel-construction deletion), `cli/internal/server/workers.go` (consumer rewrite), and a literal sweep across at least 8 test files (`workers_test.go`, `workers_e2e_test.go`, `workers_prune_test.go`, `workers_stop_test.go`, `workers_stop_propagation_test.go`, `workers_watchdog_test.go`, plus the two GraphQL test files) that all construct `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec{}` literals. - File-size signal: workers.go 1959 LOC, server.go 5248 LOC, agent_cmd.go 2245 LOC, workers_test.go 1107 LOC. Estimated change footprint comfortably \u003e500 LOC across \u003e5 files in unrelated packages. - Per execution-bead protocol size check: \"More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems\" + \"More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages\" → DECOMPOSE. ## Why I am not decomposing in this attempt The protocol's decomposition path commits the project to a particular shape (in this case: unification rather than deletion). Filing 4–6 child beads under ddx-29058e2a would advance an architectural direction that is in unresolved conflict with ddx-eccc6efb. A clean decomposition only makes sense AFTER the parent-vs-step-7 design choice is made by a human: Option A — Drop ddx-29058e2a in favor of ADR-022 step 7. The parallel-struct smell goes away by deletion, not unification. The 5 currently-dropped flags can be patched as a narrow fix on the existing struct in the meantime if the operator workflow needs them, with a note that step 7 will erase the struct. Option B — Keep ddx-29058e2a and amend / close ddx-eccc6efb. The unified spec becomes the canonical wire format; ADR-022 step 7 reduces in scope (or is closed as superseded) because the server keeps marshalling a spec, just a unified one. Option C — Sequence: land the narrow 5-flag fix now (small bead), then land ADR-022 step 7 (which deletes the struct and obsoletes the parallel-struct smell), then close ddx-29058e2a as superseded. Each option has different downstream implications (ddx-fb290074 audit scope, REACH-PROTO entry roots, where Validate/ApplyDefaults live, whether SpecVersion is needed at all). I do not have authority to pick. ## Recommended next actions for the orchestrator / triage 1. Decide which of A / B / C above is the intended path. This is a 5-minute design call, not a research task. 2. If A: close ddx-29058e2a as superseded; optionally file a small fix bead \"add MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower/FromRev to ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec + propagate through handler\" (~50 LOC, will be deleted by step 7 anyway). 3. If B: amend ddx-eccc6efb's scope (or close it), then decompose ddx-29058e2a into ~5 children: (i) new spec package + Validate/ApplyDefaults + Duration codec + reflection test, (ii) cobra rebind + parseExecuteLoopSpec + dispatch split, (iii) HTTP handler decode-into-spec rewrite + delete handler-local request struct, (iv) GraphQL adapter unification, (v) workers.go consumer migration + workers_*_test literal sweep + spec.json on-disk format flip + SpecVersion gate. 4. If C: file the narrow 5-flag fix as its own bead now; pause ddx-29058e2a until after step 7 lands, at which point most of its ACs disappear and what's left (if anything) can be re-scoped. ## What this attempt did NOT do (and why) - No code changes. The conflict is upstream of any implementation choice. - No child beads filed. Filing children would commit to the unification path; see \"Why I am not decomposing\" above. - No partial fix to the 5 dropped flags. Human triage required to resolve the design conflict before any unification work commits.\n\n## Summary\n\nThe bead asks to UNIFY the parallel ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec / cobra-flag / HTTP-request structs into one canonical `ExecuteLoopSpec` struct used at every layer. But the open ADR-022 step 7 epic (ddx-eccc6efb, status=open) takes the opposite direction: DELETE `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` entirely and replace the in-process server-spawn path with `exec ddx work` (subprocess), at which point there are no parallel structs because the server stops marshalling a spec at all — it just builds an argv vector.\n\nThese are mutually-exclusive architectural directions for the same code. Landing this bead's unification would either:\n  (a) be partially undone when ADR-022 step 7 deletes the unified struct, or\n  (b) lock in the unified-struct design and require ADR-022 step 7 to be re-scoped or abandoned.\n\nThe bead's \"SEQUENCING WITH IN-FLIGHT BEADS\" section names dc157075, 9d55601f, and a946c744, but is silent on ddx-eccc6efb / ADR-022 step 7. That gap is the load-bearing decision this bead does not make.\n\n## Evidence\n\n1. `cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49` — current `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` exists with the fields the bead lists (and is missing MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower/FromRev as the bead claims).\n2. ADR-022 step 7 (ddx-eccc6efb) is OPEN with acceptance: \"Legacy ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec serialization deletes when this lands.\" Confirmed via `ddx bead show ddx-eccc6efb`.\n3. ADR-022 step 4 (ddx-85165814) already CLOSED → `--local` is a deprecated no-op (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1576-1577`). The cobra path no longer branches to server-submit; `executeLoopWithServer` (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1986`) is unreferenced (verified: only the function definition matches; no call sites).\n4. Therefore the bead's USER-OBSERVED SYMPTOM (\"ddx work --harness claude works under --local but fails when going through the server\") cannot be reproduced via the CLI today — `--local` is a no-op and the server-submit branch from cobra is dead code. The remaining server-spawn path is via `POST /api/agent/workers/work` (`cli/internal/server/server.go:2390`) and the GraphQL `workerDispatchAdapter` (`cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:127`), both of which ADR-022 step 7 wants to delete in favor of subprocess exec.\n5. Recent ddx-eccc6efb attempt (20260503T065047-61e4ed20, result.json) was DECLINED for size and decomposed into proposed children 7a/7b/7c — those children may not yet be filed in the main repo per its own bookkeeping note, but the intent stands.\n\n## Size-check verdict (independent of the conflict)\n\nThe bead also fails the size check on its own:\n\n- 17 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems (cobra flag binding, client-side validation, HTTP handler, GraphQL adapter, workers.go consumer, on-disk persistence, backward-compat for legacy fields, forward-compat with SpecVersion, custom Duration codec, reflection-driven test, manual end-to-end run).\n- Touches at minimum: new package `cli/internal/agent/executeloop/` (struct + Validate + ApplyDefaults + custom Duration + tests), `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go` (cobra rebind + dispatch split), `cli/internal/server/server.go` (handler rewrite + handler tests), `cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go` + `graphql_adapters_default_profile_test.go` + `workers_config_test.go` (parallel-construction deletion), `cli/internal/server/workers.go` (consumer rewrite), and a literal sweep across at least 8 test files (`workers_test.go`, `workers_e2e_test.go`, `workers_prune_test.go`, `workers_stop_test.go`, `workers_stop_propagation_test.go`, `workers_watchdog_test.go`, plus the two GraphQL test files) that all construct `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec{}` literals.\n- File-size signal: workers.go 1959 LOC, server.go 5248 LOC, agent_cmd.go 2245 LOC, workers_test.go 1107 LOC. Estimated change footprint comfortably \u003e500 LOC across \u003e5 files in unrelated packages.\n- Per execution-bead protocol size check: \"More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems\" + \"More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages\" → DECOMPOSE.\n\n## Why I am not decomposing in this attempt\n\nThe protocol's decomposition path commits the project to a particular shape (in this case: unification rather than deletion). Filing 4–6 child beads under ddx-29058e2a would advance an architectural direction that is in unresolved conflict with ddx-eccc6efb. A clean decomposition only makes sense AFTER the parent-vs-step-7 design choice is made by a human:\n\n  Option A — Drop ddx-29058e2a in favor of ADR-022 step 7. The parallel-struct smell goes away by deletion, not unification. The 5 currently-dropped flags can be patched as a narrow fix on the existing struct in the meantime if the operator workflow needs them, with a note that step 7 will erase the struct.\n  Option B — Keep ddx-29058e2a and amend / close ddx-eccc6efb. The unified spec becomes the canonical wire format; ADR-022 step 7 reduces in scope (or is closed as superseded) because the server keeps marshalling a spec, just a unified one.\n  Option C — Sequence: land the narrow 5-flag fix now (small bead), then land ADR-022 step 7 (which deletes the struct and obsoletes the parallel-struct smell), then close ddx-29058e2a as superseded.\n\nEach option has different downstream implications (ddx-fb290074 audit scope, REACH-PROTO entry roots, where Validate/ApplyDefaults live, whether SpecVersion is needed at all). I do not have authority to pick.\n\n## Recommended next actions for the orchestrator / triage\n\n1. Decide which of A / B / C above is the intended path. This is a 5-minute design call, not a research task.\n2. If A: close ddx-29058e2a as superseded; optionally file a small fix bead \"add MaxCostUSD/RequestTimeout/MinPower/MaxPower/FromRev to ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec + propagate through handler\" (~50 LOC, will be deleted by step 7 anyway).\n3. If B: amend ddx-eccc6efb's scope (or close it), then decompose ddx-29058e2a into ~5 children: (i) new spec package + Validate/ApplyDefaults + Duration codec + reflection test, (ii) cobra rebind + parseExecuteLoopSpec + dispatch split, (iii) HTTP handler decode-into-spec rewrite + delete handler-local request struct, (iv) GraphQL adapter unification, (v) workers.go consumer migration + workers_*_test literal sweep + spec.json on-disk format flip + SpecVersion gate.\n4. If C: file the narrow 5-flag fix as its own bead now; pause ddx-29058e2a until after step 7 lands, at which point most of its ACs disappear and what's left (if anything) can be re-scoped.\n\n## What this attempt did NOT do (and why)\n\n- No code changes. The conflict is upstream of any implementation choice.\n- No child beads filed. Filing children would commit to the unification path; see \"Why I am not decomposing\" above.\n- No partial fix to the 5 dropped flags. The bead explicitly absorbs that narrow fix; doing it here without addressing the unification framing would muddy the bead's After a single ddx work --once cycle, ddx bead status shows non-zero Ready or Worker ready, OR ddx work focus Section A surfaces the remaining beads with concrete reason codes from the FEAT-010 taxonomy.\n4. ddx work plan --explain prints a per-bead reason code for every non-ready entry; no entry reports no_diagnosis.\n5. ddx work --once --no-auto-epic-decompose --no-auto-supersede-close --no-auto-closure-reclassify reproduces today's \"skipped N ready epic(s)\" behavior, proving the kill switches work.\n6. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... green after all child beads land.\n7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-05-16T16:55:34.976453964Z","description":"PROBLEM\nddx work currently emits \"No execution-eligible beads in the queue\" and exits when the only ready beads are epics with open children, mislabeled closure candidates, superseded leftovers, dead intermediates, or epic-of-epic chains. This violates the factory rule that ddx work must self-unstick (reliability principle P10, just added).\n\nROOT CAUSE\ndocs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md:122-167 (post-edit) and docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md \"Idle-Path Diagnosis and Auto-Remediation\" subsection (just added) now mandate diagnose+auto-remediate semantics. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1263-1315 today only reads ReadyExecutionBreakdown into result.NoReadyWorkDetail, then sleeps/exits — no diagnoser, no remediation dispatch. cli/internal/bead/lifecycle.go:218 isOrdinaryEpicContainer conflates openChildCount with totalChildCount, mislabeling 6 of 10 ready epics in the current project as containers when they are actually closure candidates.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nBuild the diagnose+auto-remediate idle path per FEAT-010 §Idle-Path Diagnosis and Auto-Remediation: per-bead Diagnose function with the closed reason taxonomy; remediator handlers for RC-1 (lifecycle classifier fix), RC-2 (supersession cascade in Close), RC-3 (dead-intermediate cleanup), RC-6 (auto-epic-decompose wrapping runPreClaimDecomposer); idle-path wiring in execute_bead_loop.go that runs diagnoser, fires matching remediators, re-scans without sleeping on success; operator surface (ddx work focus) two-section output that NEVER silently hides auto-remediable beads; ddx work plan --explain/--json flags for inspection. .ddx/beads.jsonl:97:{"acceptance":"1. `TestInstall_InTreeWritesProjectTreeAndLinks` (cli/cmd/install_test.go:21) asserts `ddx install \u003cname\u003e` in in-tree mode writes to `\u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/` and creates project-tier links under `\u003cproject\u003e/.claude/skills/\u003cname\u003e` and `\u003cproject\u003e/.agents/skills/\u003cname\u003e`.\n2. `TestInstall_ConventionModeWritesXDGProjectTree` (cli/cmd/install_test.go:73) asserts `ddx install \u003cname\u003e` in convention mode writes to `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/\u003chost\u003e/\u003cowner\u003e/\u003crepo\u003e/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/` and creates project-tier links pointing into that XDG plugins path.\n3. `TestUpdate_RespectsConventionVsInTreeMode` (cli/cmd/update_test.go:247) asserts `ddx update` (no flag) updates the project install in whichever mode is active.\n4. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/... green.\n5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"8fbb32ad36b842cefed25f048cd5511a8b64afe9","created_at":"2026-06-03T11:21:13.13129759Z","description":"PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a, slice 3 of 4)\n\nWithout `--global`, install and update must target the project's ddx root, which differs by mode: in-tree (`\u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/plugins/\u003cname\u003e`) versus convention (`${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/\u003chost\u003e/\u003cowner\u003e/\u003crepo\u003e/plugins/\u003cname\u003e`). Either way the project-tier agent skill links (`\u003cproject\u003e/.claude/skills` and `\u003cproject\u003e/.agents/skills`) must point into whichever plugins tree is active.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- `resolveInstallDirs(global=false)` (cli/cmd/install.go:184, project branch install.go:195-197) sets `installRoot = ddxroot.Path(ctx, f.WorkingDir)` and skill dirs to `\u003cworkingDir\u003e/.agents/skills` and `\u003cworkingDir\u003e/.claude/skills`. `ddxroot.Path` (cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:75) returns the in-tree `.ddx` path when `\u003cproject\u003e/.ddx` exists and the convention `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/...` path otherwise, so mode selection is implicit.\n- `installLocal` non-global branch derives `ddxRootDir := ddxroot.Path(...)` (install.go:453) for the same routing.\n- Update: the non-global path `performUpdate` (cli/cmd/update.go:204) uses `ddxroot.JoinProject(workingDir, ...)` (e.g. update.go:227) so it updates the project install in whichever mode is active; mode is not an explicit flag but follows `ddxroot`.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nEnsure `ddx install \u003cname\u003e` (no `--global`) in in-tree mode writes to `\u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/` and links `\u003cproject\u003e/.claude/skills/\u003cname\u003e` and `\u003cproject\u003e/.agents/skills/\u003cname\u003e`; in convention mode (no `\u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/`) writes to the XDG project plugins path and links the project-tier skill dirs into that path. Ensure `ddx update` (no flag) updates the project install in whichever mode is active.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- The `--global` install/update surface (slice 2).\n- `ddx init` behavior and doctor reporting (slice 4).\n- Removing either mode — in-tree and convention coexist.\n\nDEPS\n- Slice 1 (plugin lookup precedence) and ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — project paths route through `ddxroot.Path()`.","events_attachment":"ddx-2b9c26d7/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-2b9c26d7","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:plugin","area:cli","kind:feature","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas","parent:ddx-602ae31a","dep:ddx-06cbaa90"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-c7bae1d6","status":"closed","title":"project install surface: ddx install (no flag) and ddx update honor in-tree vs convention mode","updated_at":"2026-06-03T12:16:40.973821743Z","work-no-changes-count":1}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:103:{"acceptance":"1. TestReadinessChecksSchema in cli/internal/agent/readiness_schema_test.go validates every verdict form the decoder accepts (bool true, bool false, string, null, absent) against cli/internal/agent/schema/readiness-checks.schema.json AND asserts an object verdict is rejected, locking producer/consumer alignment.\n2. The producer prompt buildPreClaimIntakePrompt (cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:636-655) references the canonical schema path via the readinessChecksSchemaPath constant (cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:21), not a duplicated string literal.\n3. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... green\n4. lefthook run pre-commit passes","closing_commit_sha":"rev-ddx-2e3ad63d","created_at":"2026-05-28T09:08:21.2215798Z","description":"PROBLEM\nThe pre_claim_intake readiness payload schema can drift between the producer prompt and the Go decoder. That drift is exactly the class of bug that wedged the fizeau queue: the producer emitted readiness_checks[].verdict as a JSON bool while the consumer parsed a string, so every claim attempt failed with a warn-only rejection and the queue idled. There must be a single shared schema that both sides consume, plus a CI-run test that fails the moment the schema and decoder diverge.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- The producer prompt references the schema only by a path string in prose: cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:639 (buildPreClaimIntakePrompt) writes \"Canonical schema: \"+readinessChecksSchemaPath, and the constant lives at cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:21. Because the prompt text and the schema file are independent, instructions can drift from the schema with nothing catching it.\n- The consumer decoder accepts bool/string/null/absent verdict forms: readinessVerdict.UnmarshalJSON at cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:97-125 and preClaimReadinessChecksPayload.UnmarshalJSON at cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:386-416.\n- The schema verdict union is cli/internal/agent/schema/readiness-checks.schema.json:44-50 (oneOf boolean/string/null). Nothing fails CI if someone tightens the schema, or changes the decoder, so that the two diverge.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nKeep cli/internal/agent/schema/readiness-checks.schema.json as the single source of truth consumed by both the producer prompt (via the readinessChecksSchemaPath constant) and the consumer decoder. Ensure a CI-run test validates every verdict form the decoder accepts against the schema and asserts that an object verdict is rejected, so any tightening of the schema or change to the decoder that breaks alignment fails the suite. Confirm the producer prompt references the canonical path constant rather than a duplicated literal.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Do NOT implement the runtime same-fingerprint warn escalation guard (owned by the sibling child bead).\n- Do NOT change the verdict coercion semantics (bool true-\u003epass, bool false-\u003efail, string-\u003elowercased, null/absent-\u003eempty).\n- Do NOT introduce go.mod replace directives.\n\nDEPENDENCIES: no deps.","events_attachment":"ddx-2e3ad63d/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-2e3ad63d","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:execute-bead","area:pre-claim","kind:reliability","observability","priority:reliability","decomposed","needs-human-decomposition","triage:no-changes-unjustified"],"parent":"ddx-fd02cf6e","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"status":"closed","title":"Lock readiness_checks schema as the shared producer/consumer source of truth with a CI drift gate","updated_at":"2026-05-28T10:31:56.276408092Z","work-no-changes-count":1}

The fixtures must be self-contained and must not reference Niflheim paths as required inputs.\n2. `TestExecuteBeadWorker_NiflheimEvidence_EmptyReviewResultCannotClose` proves a non-skipped success path with `cycle_trace.review_result={}` or an equivalent empty `ReviewResult` cannot close a bead and records `review_error` or operator-attention with a non-empty reason.\n3. `TestExecuteBeadWorker_NiflheimEvidence_NilReviewerRequiresExplicitSkip` updates the current nil-reviewer behavior so `Reviewer: nil` only closes when an explicit durable no-review/skip reason is present; otherwise it fails review gating.\n4. `TestExecuteBeadLoop_NiflheimEvidence_LandCoordinationFailureDoesNotRerunImplementation` simulates both observed details (`cannot lock ref refs/heads/main` after a candidate/merge is already landed, and `landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s`) and asserts the action is reconcile/retry-land/operator-attention, not a fresh implementation run and not model escalation.\n5. `TestExecuteBeadLoop_NiflheimEvidence_DecomposedParentIsExecutionIneligible` simulates `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale` with `orchestrator_action: decompose`, child bead IDs, and `outcome_reason=decomposed`; it asserts the parent is marked execution-ineligible/proposed or dependency-waiting according to policy and that no further implementation attempt or min-power escalation is scheduled.\n6. `TestWorkRetryEscalation_NiflheimEvidence_OnlySemanticFailuresRaiseMinPower` proves only semantic acceptance/review failures can increase requested min power/profile. Land coordination, repeated pre-claim system warnings, decomposed-parent outcomes, and dirty generated DDx evidence must not raise min power.\n7. `TestExecuteBeadResult_NiflheimEvidence_CycleTraceCarriesDecisionAudit` proves result JSON/event bodies include `failure_class`, `retry_action`, `escalation_count`, requested/actual routing facts, review status/verdict or explicit skip reason, and land/reconcile status for the Niflheim scenario families.\n8. Existing related behavior from `ddx-2965925e`, `ddx-b2846610`, and `ddx-d67f124e` remains covered; this bead either reuses their helpers or waits for those dependencies, and does not add duplicate low-level implementations.\n9. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'NiflheimEvidence|EmptyReviewResult|NilReviewer|LandCoordination|DecomposedParent|RetryEscalation|CycleTraceCarriesDecisionAudit' -count=1` passes.\n10. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -count=1` passes.\n11. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","created_at":"2026-06-06T15:55:41.332809555Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-334c0958","depends_on_id":"ddx-b2846610","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-06-06T15:55:52Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-334c0958","depends_on_id":"ddx-2965925e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-06-06T15:55:53Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-334c0958","depends_on_id":"ddx-d67f124e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-06-06T15:55:55Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM\nA live Niflheim worker audit on 2026-06-06 showed that `ddx work` is not yet a stable release-grade autonomous worker. Strong-model escalation exists, but it is not reliably tied to the class of failure. The worker spent repeated attempts on structural or system failures that stronger models cannot fix, while automatic review evidence was empty on attempts that were recorded as successful. This violates the DDx engineering principle \"Cheapest Viable, Escalate on Evidence\" (docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:39-45): review, runtime, or retry evidence should determine escalation, and only lower-power inability to meet acceptance criteria should push to stronger routes. It also threatens FEAT-010 task-execution reliability because beads can be retried, escalated, or closed without a typed, auditable reason.\n\nThis bead is a Niflheim incident/regression closure bead. It is not a duplicate of the narrower queue items listed under INTERSECTIONS; instead it captures the external evidence DDx workers will not otherwise have, and requires a DDx-side regression suite proving those observed scenarios classify correctly before rerun, escalation, operator attention, or close.\n\nBACKGROUND AND OBSERVED NIFLHEIM EVIDENCE\nThe evidence was gathered from Niflheim worker logs and result bundles, but those files are not load-bearing for this bead. The relevant facts are copied here because an isolated DDx worker cannot access the Niflheim repository or `/tmp` logs.\n\n1. Decomposed parent kept consuming attempts and escalated to a stronger model anyway.\n- Niflheim bead: `niflheim-64217cc5`, title \"Instrument cold replicator with structured per-operation S3 tracing\".\n- Observed result bundle: `.ddx/executions/20260606T152006-a4ca7254/result.json` plus worker logs from 2026-06-05/2026-06-06.\n- Routing showed escalation exists: repeated routes started at `gpt-5.4-mini` with requested min powers 5, 7, 8, and 9, then reached `gpt-5.5` at requested min power 10.\n- Metrics on the bead showed `attempt_count=24`, `total_tokens=19371353`, and average duration about `244112ms`.\n- Despite triage recommending `close_decomposed_or_mark_execution_ineligible`, retries continued until the latest event recorded:\n  `mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale`\n  `powerClass=standard`\n  `rationale: status: open`\n  `orchestrator_action: decompose`\n  `reason: parent bead exceeds the Step 0 size threshold and is already decomposed into focused child beads for tracing, counters, tests, and runbook work.`\n  `child_beads: niflheim-f03c61a2, niflheim-df407aa8, niflheim-7a421ede, niflheim-0bbed6c9`\n  `outcome_reason=decomposed`\n- Expected DDx behavior: once the worker has reliable evidence that the bead is a decomposed parent or execution-ineligible parent, no further implementation attempt or model escalation should occur. It should mark execution-ineligible/proposed/operator-attention according to policy and move on.\n\n2. Land/evidence coordination failures were treated like implementation failure and retried expensively.\n- Niflheim bead: `niflheim-c75aba30`, title \"Materializer per-merge memory footprint OOMs even at concurrent_limit=8\".\n- Two expensive attempts both used `gpt-5.4-mini`; no useful stronger-model escalation was observed.\n- Metrics showed `attempt_count=2`, `total_tokens=65513510`, and average duration about `3306461.5ms`.\n- First attempt result: `.ddx/executions/20260606T113550-7b29ee42/result.json`.\n  - `status=execution_failed`, `outcome=error`.\n  - Implementation result commit was `7aaeb0712...`; a merge commit `2c6f4d6e6` was later observed as an ancestor of HEAD, but the result still recorded a land coordination failure.\n  - Error detail:\n    `land coordination failed: land advancer: restoring refs/heads/main to ba2d3059f14e834dcb147f2a6db3b294d83b0fac after evidence commit failed: git update-ref refs/heads/main: fatal: update_ref failed for ref 'refs/heads/main': cannot lock ref 'refs/heads/main': is at 5a176b332b182f2a959614f63769d835c47ffd97 but expected 2c6f4d6e6961b8a93742cde66bceec056934655a`\n  - `cycle_trace[0].final_decision=\"success\"` and `cycle_trace[0].review_result={}`.\n- Second attempt result: `.ddx/executions/20260606T142223-94f8f0f8/result.json`.\n  - `status=execution_failed`, `outcome=error`.\n  - Error detail:\n    `land coordination failed: land advancer: landing worktree has staged changes after waiting 2s: A .ddx/executions/20260606T150832-af40450b/result.json`\n  - `cycle_trace[0].final_decision=\"success\"` and `cycle_trace[0].review_result={}`.\n- At observation time the worker had claimed it again (`20260606T152024-a21d8c54`) and edited six files in a fresh isolated worktree:\n  `crates/niflheim-core/src/config/mod.rs`, `crates/niflheim-core/src/config/tests.rs`, `crates/niflheim-core/src/config/types.rs`, `crates/niflheim-delta-writer/src/materializer/config.rs`, `crates/niflheim-delta-writer/src/materializer/executor.rs`, and `crates/niflheim-delta-writer/src/materializer/tests.rs`.\n- Expected DDx behavior: land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures are system or coordination failures, not model-quality failures. The retry policy should reconcile a successfully landed candidate when possible, retry only the land/evidence step when safe, or park/operator-attention the bead when the landing worktree is dirty. It should not blindly rerun implementation at the same or stronger route.\n\n3. Pre-claim intake/system dirt caused repeated warning churn instead of a clear operator lane.\n- Niflheim bead: `niflheim-a62d31ce`, title \"Publish competitive benchmark artifacts and provenance metadata\".\n- A live worker repeatedly ran readiness/intake. The log showed readiness timeout after five minutes, then:\n  `preserved pre-dispatch implementation changes in /home/erik/Projects/niflheim under refs/ddx/pre-dispatch/20260606T153517-f7a32618; released niflheim-a62d31ce and continuing watch. dirty paths: .ddx/harness-sessions/-2929681.json. recover with git stash apply refs/ddx/pre-dispatch/20260606T153517-f7a32618`\n- Bead events included `pre_claim_intake.warn` with timeout/system-unready reason and generic suggested action.\n- Expected DDx behavior: repeated same/systemic pre-claim warnings and dirty generated DDx artifacts should become an operator-visible/system-unready state after the configured threshold, not an indefinite poll/release loop.\n\n4. Automatic reviews were not forcing meaningful quality improvement.\n- Recent Niflheim \"success\" attempts had empty review results:\n  - `niflheim-9dea95da`, result `.ddx/executions/20260606T043655-845c77ee/result.json`: closed success, `cycle_trace.review_result={}`, `final_decision=\"success\"`, model `gpt-5.4-mini`, result commit `78e51a168...`.\n  - `niflheim-da4e1311`, result `.ddx/executions/20260606T085358-d80ada3e/result.json`: closed success, `cycle_trace.review_result={}`, `final_decision=\"success\"`, model `gpt-5.4-mini`, result commit `d43be3a3...`.\n  - `niflheim-c75aba30` attempts listed above: `cycle_trace.final_decision=\"success\"` with `review_result={}` despite overall land failures.\n- Manual Claude review earlier improved bead wording and completeness, but the automatic worker path did not show enforceable per-AC review evidence.\n- Expected DDx behavior: unless review is explicitly skipped with a durable reason (`runtime.NoReview` or a valid `review:skip` + reason policy), an empty review result must be a review error or operator-attention signal. It must not be treated as approval, success, or evidence of quality improvement.\n\nROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE\n- `cli/internal/agent/failclass/failclass.go:103-142` contains the normative routing table: only `OutcomeGenuineFailure` can escalate; dirty worktree, transport, quota, auth, setup, timeout, and merge-conflict do not escalate. The live Niflheim behavior shows execute-bead/work outcomes are not consistently reduced to this typed table before retry/escalation decisions.\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:305-309` wraps `LandingAdvancer` errors as generic `land advancer` errors. The caller can record an overall `execution_failed` without distinguishing landed-candidate reconciliation, ref/index contention, staged evidence dirt, or dirty landing worktree from implementation failure.\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1967-2072` emits pre-claim timeout/system-unready outcomes as warn-only by default and continues implementation when strict intake blocking is off. This permits repeated same-fingerprint/system warnings to look like a live worker instead of a system-unready/operator lane.\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2175-2315` handles `PreClaimIntakeTooLargeDecomposed`, but missing/empty decomposition paths can warn and continue. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3826-3831` can recognize execution-ineligible/decomposed state only after the bead has the right metadata. The Niflheim parent evidence shows structural/decomposed-parent outcomes can still reach repeated implementation attempts and power escalation.\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3042-3138` treats success disposition/status as closeable once candidate-cycle review/landing has allowed success, and the old post-merge review is optional. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_test.go:799-823` explicitly proves a worker with `Reviewer: nil` can skip review and close the bead. `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:168-193` defines `ReviewResult` with all fields optional except the zero-value enum field; no invariant rejects `{}` as a success-path review artifact.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- Add compact Niflheim-style regression fixtures under `cli/internal/agent/testdata/niflheim_worker_evidence/` or equivalent. The fixtures should encode the four scenario families above: empty review success, land coordination/ref-lock/staged-evidence failure, decomposed-parent no-changes rationale with child beads, and repeated pre-claim system-unready warning. Do not depend on Niflheim checkout paths.\n- Introduce or tighten a single execute-bead retry classification boundary that maps attempt result, land result, review result, no-changes rationale, and pre-claim intake outcome into exactly one action: close, retry same tier, escalate min power/profile, reconcile land/evidence, execution-ineligible/proposed, or operator-attention. Wire this boundary into `ddx work`/`ddx try` so `failclass.Classify` is not merely a standalone helper.\n- Enforce review evidence on success. For non-skipped success paths, `cycle_trace.review_result` or the equivalent result field must contain a non-empty verdict, per-AC evidence or documented reason, reviewer routing/session metadata when available, and a classification. `{}` is invalid and must prevent close or produce a review-error/operator-attention event. Explicit review skips remain allowed only when they are durable, reasoned, and visible in result JSON.\n- Classify land/ref/index/evidence-staging failures separately from implementation/model failures. If the candidate commit or merge is already landed or is an ancestor of the target, reconcile and close/continue without rerunning implementation. If the landing worktree has staged generated evidence or DDx metadata, retry/preserve the land/evidence step or move to operator attention according to the existing local reliability policy; do not escalate the model for this class.\n- Classify decomposed-parent/no-changes rationale as execution-ineligible or operator work before another implementation attempt. Stronger models cannot fix parent beads that are already split into executable children.\n- Make worker result JSON and event bodies carry enough audit fields to explain the decision: `failure_class`, `retry_action`, `escalation_count`, requested/actual power/profile/model, review status/verdict, and land/reconcile status.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Do not change Fizeau route selection or hard-code concrete provider/model names. DDx should continue to pass power/profile intent and let Fizeau choose concrete routes.\n- Do not edit Niflheim beads or Niflheim implementation code.\n- Do not duplicate the low-level index-contention fix tracked by `ddx-2965925e`; reuse or align with it.\n- Do not duplicate the post-commit review/land timeout work tracked by `ddx-b2846610`; this bead requires typed classification and review evidence, not another timeout implementation.\n- Do not duplicate the repeated pre-claim warn operator-attention bead `ddx-d67f124e`; this bead uses the Niflheim observation as cross-scenario regression coverage.\n- Do not implement a full operator-lane product workflow beyond the state transitions/events needed to keep the worker from blind retry or false close.\n\nINTERSECTIONS\n- Parent: `ddx-3106674f` (multi-worker hardening and local reliability gates). This bead is the external Niflheim regression/incident closure layer under that reliability epic.\n- Dependency: `ddx-2965925e` covers tracker commit index contention. This bead must not reimplement that fix; it must prove land/ref/index/evidence failures classify separately from model failure once the low-level path is hardened.\n- Dependency: `ddx-b2846610` covers bounded post-commit review/land. This bead must still require typed review/land outcomes after the timeout wedge is fixed.\n- Dependency: `ddx-d67f124e` covers same-fingerprint pre-claim warning escalation. This bead adds the Niflheim `niflheim-a62d31ce` family as regression evidence.\n- Related but not a dependency: `ddx-e1a576a7` is an execution-ineligible decomposed parent and a local precedent for the `niflheim-64217cc5` class.\n- Related quality framework: `ddx-a9d130d0` and `ddx-ac10e7af` own broader bead lifecycle/operator-lane policy; this bead is concrete worker behavior and evidence gating.","id":"ddx-334c0958","issue_type":"bug","labels":["phase:iterate","area:agent","area:execute-bead","area:workers","area:review","kind:bug","kind:reliability","severity:critical","spec:FEAT-010","spec:TP-021","adr:024","incident:niflheim-2026-06-06","prevention"],"parent":"ddx-3106674f","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"FEAT-010","status":"open","title":"execute-bead worker: gate success on review evidence and classify Niflheim retry failures","updated_at":"2026-06-06T15:55:55.96445459Z"}

TestPluginLookup_FallsBackToGlobal (internal/registry/resolve_test.go) asserts that when the project copy is absent but the global copy exists, ResolvePlugin returns the global path with layer==\"global\".\n3. Both tests use an isolated temp HOME/XDG fixture and do not touch the real user home.\n4. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/registry/... green.\n5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"13eaa6a7758297f2001118d4533a33c70a272e9d","created_at":"2026-06-03T17:21:03.026972073Z","description":"PROBLEM (Principle 5 of epic ddx-602ae31a)\nPlugin content can live in two tiers — per-project (ddxroot.Path()/plugins/\u003cname\u003e) and machine-wide global (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/\u003cname\u003e). Without a single authoritative resolver, install/update/init/doctor each reinvent path logic and drift.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- internal/registry/resolve.go:29 `ResolvePlugin(ctx, projectRoot, name)` is the precedence resolver: project path computed at resolve.go:30, global at resolve.go:35, baked-in fallback at resolve.go:44. It must be the only place precedence is decided and must be covered by named tests so install/init/doctor can rely on it.\n- internal/registry/resolve.go:15 `GlobalPluginDir(name)` returns ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/\u003cname\u003e via ddxroot.GlobalDir(); the project path routes through ddxroot.Path() (in-tree vs convention mode handled there).\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n1. Do not alter install/update/init/doctor call sites here beyond confirming they call `ResolvePlugin` (those are covered by sibling children).\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Removing the per-project install tier (both tiers coexist).\n- Migrating ~/.config/ddx/ legacy paths.\n- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.\n- install/update/init/doctor wiring (sibling children).\n\nDEPS\nddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — ResolvePlugin routes through both ddxroot.Path() and ddxroot.GlobalDir(); both must be agreed-on before this lands.\n\nGOVERNING ARTIFACTS\n- docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-027-skill-install-topology.md (precedence decision record).\n- CLAUDE.md \"Persona System\" three-layer precedence note.","events_attachment":"ddx-3478153c/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-3478153c","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:config","area:plugin","kind:feature","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-e6291b32","status":"closed","title":"registry: runtime plugin lookup precedence (project → global → baked-in)","updated_at":"2026-06-03T17:23:53.361549509Z","work-no-changes-count":1}

This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.\n\nROOT CAUSE\nThe decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:\n\n  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --\u003e child_A\n  e29892a4 -- parent --\u003e child_B\n  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           \u003c- BAD: parent is also a dep\n  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  \u003c- BAD\n\nThe parent stays open until its children close. .ddx/beads.jsonl:131:{"acceptance":"1. The prose checker no longer hard-fails on a non-exact-match Vale version: it either accepts a compatible range or auto-installs the pinned Vale into a ddx cache dir.\n2. A test in cli/internal/docprose (e.g. TestValeVersionAcceptsCompatibleRange or TestValeBootstrapResolvesBinary) covers the new behavior without requiring a preinstalled exact vale.\n3. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/docprose/... green\n4. lefthook run pre-commit passes","closing_commit_sha":"rev-ddx-38c58f5b","created_at":"2026-05-28T15:00:46.884617521Z","description":"PROBLEM: The ddx prose checker requires an EXACT Vale version string and neither accepts a compatible range nor installs Vale itself. Operators without precisely that build are hard-blocked, with no bootstrap.\nROOT CAUSE: cli/internal/docprose/vale_runner.go:13 SupportedValeVersion=\"vale version 3.13.0\"; cli/cmd/doctor.go:24 const supportedValeVersion + :631 errors when version != supportedValeVersion (exact-string equality); vale_runner.go:62 exec.LookPath(\"vale\") only — no install/bootstrap. No version-range tolerance and no self-install.\nPROPOSED FIX: either (a) accept a documented compatible version range (e.g. \u003e= 3.13.0 within the 3.x line) instead of exact-string equality, or (b) bootstrap the pinned Vale (download the pinned release into a ddx-managed cache dir and invoke that) so the check is self-sufficient on a clean machine. Prefer (b) with (a) as fallback.\nNON-SCOPE: changing Vale styles/rules.","events_attachment":"ddx-38c58f5b/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-38c58f5b","issue_type":"bug","labels":["phase:2","area:cli","area:checks","kind:bug","operator-ux","reliability"],"priority":2,"schema_version":1,"status":"closed","title":"doctor/prose: Vale check requires exact pinned version and self-installs nothing — make it flexible or bootstrap Vale","updated_at":"2026-05-28T16:53:19.423005243Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:133:{"acceptance":"1. `TestRunStatePathUsesDDxRoot` proves agent run-state/evidence helpers resolve through `ddxroot.Path` for a convention-root project.\n2. `TestExecStoreUsesDDxRoot` proves exec attachment and run-store paths resolve through `ddxroot.Path` instead of `projectRoot/.ddx`.\n3. `rg 'filepath\\.Join\\([^)]*\"\\.ddx\"' cli/internal/agent cli/internal/exec cli/internal/persona cli/internal/workerstatus cli/internal/processmetrics cli/internal/agentmetrics --type go | grep -v '^cli/internal/ddxroot/'` returns zero hits.\n4. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/exec/... ./internal/persona/... ./internal/workerstatus/... ./internal/processmetrics/... ./internal/agentmetrics/...` passes.\n5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","closing_commit_sha":"029d3225801286508aa0a0146e0bfb4686ba9ddd","created_at":"2026-05-15T06:00:35.042921557Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-3bbf066b","depends_on_id":"ddx-e4c3d791","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-15T06:00:57Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM\nThe execution/runtime packages still build project-state paths with literal `.ddx` joins, so run-state, evidence, worker liveness, and exec attachments will not follow convention-mode roots even after the CLI layer is fixed.\n\nROOT CAUSE\nRepresentative direct joins remain in `cli/internal/agent/run_state.go:57`, `cli/internal/agent/execution_cleanup.go:216`, `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1790`, `cli/internal/exec/store.go:54`, `cli/internal/exec/bead_runtime.go:421`, `cli/internal/workerstatus/liveness.go:42`, `cli/internal/processmetrics/processmetrics.go:667`, `cli/internal/agentmetrics/loader.go:235`, and `cli/internal/persona/loader.go:77`.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- Add package-local helpers that route runtime/project-state paths through `ddxroot.Path` for `internal/agent`, `internal/exec`, `internal/persona`, `internal/workerstatus`, `internal/processmetrics`, and `internal/agentmetrics`.\n- Replace the remaining literal `.ddx` joins in those packages.\n- Add focused tests proving run-state/evidence and exec attachment paths resolve against the DDx root chosen by `ddxroot.Path`.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- `cli/cmd`, config/git, and server/graphql packages.\n- Test-only files and the final full-tree hook gate.\n\nDEPS\n- No deps.","events_attachment":"ddx-3bbf066b/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-3bbf066b","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:config"," .ddx/beads.jsonl:137:{"acceptance":"1. cli/internal/bead/store.go exposes Init(ctx context.Context) error and GenID(ctx context.Context) (string, error), and those two methods no longer parse context through storeCallContext.\n2. cli/internal/bead/backend.go exposes BeadInitializer.Init(ctx context.Context) error.\n3. TestInitGlobal_CreatesAgentTierLinks asserts `ddx init --global` installs the default ddx plugin to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx and that ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx point into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/, and that ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml is written with convention defaults.\n2. TestInitProject_DoesNotInstallPlugins asserts `ddx init` (no flag, in-project) creates \u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/ and its manifest but does not write under plugins/ — the plugin lazy-resolves from the global tier.\n3. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/... green.\n4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"4664c3d9dbff6177e63eb5dde486415159e2413f","created_at":"2026-06-03T05:42:23.055248099Z","description":"PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)\n\nThere is no first-time machine setup command, and `ddx init` bundles plugin install with manifest creation, forcing a plugin write into every newly initialized project instead of lazy-resolving from the global tier.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- cli/cmd/init.go bundles plugin install with manifest creation; there is no `--global` umbrella path that installs the default `ddx` plugin globally and writes global config.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n1. `ddx init --global` is the umbrella first-time-setup command: installs the default `ddx` plugin globally to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx (via GlobalPluginDir), creates agent-tier links (~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx pointing into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/), and writes ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults.\n2. `ddx init` (no flag) creates \u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/ and its manifest but does NOT install plugins; plugins are lazy-resolved on first use, preferring the project tier over global per the registry precedence bead.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Install command `--global` flag (sibling install bead).\n- Update command and doctor reporting (sibling update bead).\n- The runtime precedence implementation (registry bead).\n- Removing the existing per-project install (both modes coexist).\n- Migrating legacy ~/.config/ddx paths.\n- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.\n\nDEPS\n- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution).\n- Registry resolution sibling bead (GlobalPluginDir) — init --global writes through it.\n\nGOVERNING ARTIFACTS\n- Epic ddx-602ae31a, Principle 2.","events_attachment":"ddx-3ecfd11d/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-3ecfd11d","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:config","area:cli","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas","kind:feature","phase:3"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-b321fc9b","status":"closed","title":"init: add `ddx init --global` umbrella setup and make plain `ddx init` skip plugin install","updated_at":"2026-06-03T06:52:49.876373361Z"}

Current state of the foundational Store methods (cli/internal/bead/store.go): Init(args ...any) error                                  // line 149 GenID(args ...any) (string, error)                       // line 167 ReadAll(args ...any) ([]Bead, error)                     // line 182 ReadAllFiltered(args ...any) ([]Bead, error)             // line 224 Create(args ...any) error                                // line 518 Get(args ...any) (*Bead, error)                          // line 598 Update(args ...any) error                                // line 627 Close(args ...any) error                                 // line 1426 And in archive.go: GetWithArchive(id string) (*Bead, error)                 // line 172 Each variadic method extracts ctx via storeCallContext(args) and accepts either `s.Init()` or `s.Init(context.Background())` today. 2. AC1 requires these methods to take `ctx context.Context` as a typed first parameter (matching the BeadEventReader / BeadQueries shape already in cli/internal/bead/backend.go:91-114). Any concrete signature satisfying AC1 — `Init(ctx context.Context) error`, `Init(ctx context.Context, args ...any) error`, etc. — rejects the current zero-arg `s.Init()` callsites that exist in external packages. 3. The bead's PROPOSED FIX and NON-SCOPE explicitly forbid touching external callers (\"Do not touch the external caller sweep in this bead\"; \"NON-SCOPE: Caller migration in cli/cmd, cli/internal/agent, cli/internal/server, cli/internal/escalation, and metrics packages.\") and AC4 requires \"no caller-package changes in this bead.\" 4. AC3 requires `lefthook run pre-commit` to pass, which runs `go build -buildvcs=false -v ./...` from cli/ (lefthook.yml:122-129). That build covers every package in the module, including all the external caller packages NON-SCOPE forbids touching. 5. Concrete external breakage if AC1 is implemented today (`grep -rE '\\b(store|s)\\.(Init|Create|Update|Get|Close|ReadAll|GenID|GetWithArchive|ReadAllFiltered)\\(' cli/ --include='*.go'`): - 174 Go files reference these Store methods. - 73 distinct files outside cli/internal/bead/ use the zero-arg `store.Init()` form (e.g. cli/cmd/ac_test.go:37, cli/cmd/bead_human_test.go:20, cli/internal/agent/..., cli/internal/server/...). - Production-code stragglers without ctx exist too: cli/cmd/bead.go:414  s.GetWithArchive(args[0]) cli/cmd/bead.go:1281 s.Close(id) cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4485 store.Get(id) cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4811 store.Update(beadID, ...) `go build ./...` and the lefthook go-build hook will fail at every one of those callsites the instant Init/Get/Update/Close/ReadAll/GenID/ GetWithArchive/ReadAllFiltered become non-variadic ctx-first. There is no signature shape that satisfies AC1 while preserving all current call patterns (Go does not allow same-name method overloads on a single receiver type). 6. Step 0 size envelope is also exceeded for the only resolution that preserves AC1+AC3: the necessary mechanical caller sweep crosses ~170 files in five packages (cmd, internal/agent, internal/server, internal/attemptmetrics, internal/agentmetrics) — well past the \"~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages\" guardrail. That is exactly the sweep that NON-SCOPE excludes, and the parent split (TD-027 / ddx-464feba2) was rejected for the same reason. Operator action requested: Re-scope this bead to one of: (a) Add the caller-package migration to scope (delete the NON-SCOPE block and AC4's \"no caller-package changes\"). This becomes the original TD-027 sweep that previously failed as too large; if pursued, expect a multi-file diff across cli/cmd, cli/internal/agent, cli/internal/server, cli/internal/attemptmetrics, cli/internal/agentmetrics. (b) Replace AC3 with a narrower verification (e.g. \"go test ./internal/bead/... and go vet ./internal/bead/... pass\"), and explicitly accept that `go build ./...` and lefthook pre-commit will be red until follow-up caller-migration beads land. The follow-up beads would then re-establish AC3 once the sweep is complete. (c) Replace AC1 with an additive shape that does not rename or retype the existing variadic methods — e.g. \"add new ctx-first Store methods with distinct names (InitCtx / GetCtx / …) so the legacy variadic surface stays in place during the migration\", then a follow-up bead deletes the variadic methods after the caller sweep finishes. This satisfies the spirit of TD-027 (introducing the typed ctx-first surface in the bead package first) without breaking the build outside the package. No code changes were made on this attempt.
