Current state of the foundational Store methods (cli/internal/bead/store.go): Init(args ...any) error                                  // line 149 GenID(args ...any) (string, error)                       // line 167 ReadAll(args ...any) ([]Bead, error)                     // line 182 ReadAllFiltered(args ...any) ([]Bead, error)             // line 224 Create(args ...any) error                                // line 518 Get(args ...any) (*Bead, error)                          // line 598 Update(args ...any) error                                // line 627 Close(args ...any) error                                 // line 1426 And in archive.go: GetWithArchive(id string) (*Bead, error)                 // line 172 Each variadic method extracts ctx via storeCallContext(args) and accepts either `s.Init()` or `s.Init(context.Background())` today. 2. AC1 requires these methods to take `ctx context.Context` as a typed first parameter (matching the BeadEventReader / BeadQueries shape already in cli/internal/bead/backend.go:91-114). Any concrete signature satisfying AC1 — `Init(ctx context.Context) error`, `Init(ctx context.Context, args ...any) error`, etc. — rejects the current zero-arg `s.Init()` callsites that exist in external packages. 3. The bead's PROPOSED FIX and NON-SCOPE explicitly forbid touching external callers (\"Do not touch the external caller sweep in this bead\"; \"NON-SCOPE: Caller migration in cli/cmd, cli/internal/agent, cli/internal/server, cli/internal/escalation, and metrics packages.\") and AC4 requires \"no caller-package changes in this bead.\" 4. AC3 requires `lefthook run pre-commit` to pass, which runs `go build -buildvcs=false -v ./...` from cli/ (lefthook.yml:122-129). That build covers every package in the module, including all the external caller packages NON-SCOPE forbids touching. 5. Concrete external breakage if AC1 is implemented today (`grep -rE '\\b(store|s)\\.(Init|Create|Update|Get|Close|ReadAll|GenID|GetWithArchive|ReadAllFiltered)\\(' cli/ --include='*.go'`): - 174 Go files reference these Store methods. - 73 distinct files outside cli/internal/bead/ use the zero-arg `store.Init()` form (e.g. cli/cmd/ac_test.go:37, cli/cmd/bead_human_test.go:20, cli/internal/agent/..., cli/internal/server/...). - Production-code stragglers without ctx exist too: cli/cmd/bead.go:414  s.GetWithArchive(args[0]) cli/cmd/bead.go:1281 s.Close(id) cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4485 store.Get(id) cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4811 store.Update(beadID, ...) `go build ./...` and the lefthook go-build hook will fail at every one of those callsites the instant Init/Get/Update/Close/ReadAll/GenID/ GetWithArchive/ReadAllFiltered become non-variadic ctx-first. There is no signature shape that satisfies AC1 while preserving all current call patterns (Go does not allow same-name method overloads on a single receiver type). 6. Step 0 size envelope is also exceeded for the only resolution that preserves AC1+AC3: the necessary mechanical caller sweep crosses ~170 files in five packages (cmd, internal/agent, internal/server, internal/attemptmetrics, internal/agentmetrics) — well past the \"~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages\" guardrail. That is exactly the sweep that NON-SCOPE excludes, and the parent split (TD-027 / ddx-464feba2) was rejected for the same reason. Operator action requested: Re-scope this bead to one of: (a) Add the caller-package migration to scope (delete the NON-SCOPE block and AC4's \"no caller-package changes\"). This becomes the original TD-027 sweep that previously failed as too large; if pursued, expect a multi-file diff across cli/cmd, cli/internal/agent, cli/internal/server, cli/internal/attemptmetrics, cli/internal/agentmetrics. (b) Replace AC3 with a narrower verification (e.g. \"go test ./internal/bead/... and go vet ./internal/bead/... pass\"), and explicitly accept that `go build ./...` and lefthook pre-commit will be red until follow-up caller-migration beads land. The follow-up beads would then re-establish AC3 once the sweep is complete. (c) Replace AC1 with an additive shape that does not rename or retype the existing variadic methods — e.g. \"add new ctx-first Store methods with distinct names (InitCtx / GetCtx / …) so the legacy variadic surface stays in place during the migration\", then a follow-up bead deletes the variadic methods after the caller sweep finishes. This satisfies the spirit of TD-027 (introducing the typed ctx-first surface in the bead package first) without breaking the build outside the package. No code changes were made on this attempt. Request was triaged strictly against the bead body — no /tmp scratch files, no work outside the worktree.","work-last-status":"no_changes_operator_required","work-no-changes-count":1,"work-suggested-action":"review and accept, split, block, or cancel this proposed work"}

When that happens in the same run that already exhibits a parent-back-edge state, the operator only sees a soft warning instead of the structural failure that actually occurred.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n`cli/internal/agent/lifecycle_dispatch.go:130-165` rejects root mutations but does not include the offending bead ID in the failure shape, and `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:595` plus the surrounding loop code consume the error as a warning path. The result is a muted signal even though the run is already in a bad structural state.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nWhen the parent-back-edge state and a root-mutation rejection coincide, upgrade the log/event severity from warning to error and attach the offending bead ID to the emitted message. .ddx/beads.jsonl:147:{"acceptance":"1. Add `TestExecuteBeadWorkerCannotMutatePrimaryGitConfig` or equivalent, using a real primary repo plus execute-bead worktree, that contaminates the worker attempt with commands such as `git config --local core.bare true`, `git config --local user.email fixture@ddx.test`, and a nested test subprocess with Git-local env vars set.\n2. The test asserts after the attempt that the primary checkout satisfies `git status --short`, has no `core.bare`, has no stray `core.worktree`, and does not contain fixture `user.name`/`user.email`.\n3. The fix ensures linked worktree attempts have per-worktree config isolation and/or scrubbed Git env for all agent/test subprocesses that DDx launches.\n4. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... ./internal/git/... -run \"TestExecuteBeadWorkerCannotMutatePrimaryGitConfig|TestGitFixtureHelpersStripHookEnv|TestCheckGitRepoHealthDetectsCoreBare\" -count=1` passes.\n5. `sh scripts/git-config-health.sh` passes after the new regression runs.\n6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","created_at":"2026-05-15T05:40:54.944068779Z","description":"PROBLEM\nDuring live `ddx work --watch --harness codex --model gpt-5.4` on bead `ddx-fa6eedcc` at 2026-05-15 05:38 ET, the primary checkout `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx` flipped to `core.bare=true`. `git status --short` in the primary checkout failed with `fatal: this operation must be run in a work tree`; `git config --local --get core.bare` returned `true`. I repaired the local checkout with `git config --unset-all --local core.bare`, but worker attempts must not be able to corrupt the operator's primary repo config.\n\nROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE\n`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:391-396` creates linked worktrees with `git worktree add --detach`; `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1242-1247` launches the worker in that isolated worktree. Doctor knows the corruption class at `cli/cmd/doctor.go:1035-1044` (`extensions.worktreeConfig` disabled can corrupt shared config), but this incident occurred while the main repo had `extensions.worktreeConfig=true`, so the execute-bead isolation contract still lacks an end-to-end guard that worker commands and tests cannot mutate the primary checkout's `.git/config` or `.git/config.worktree`.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nAdd an execute-bead regression that snapshots the primary checkout git config before a worker attempt, runs a script/agent attempt that intentionally tries to set `core.bare=true` and fixture identity from inside the attempt worktree and test subprocesses, and proves the primary checkout remains a valid worktree with no `core.bare`, no stray `core.worktree`, and no leaked fixture user config after the attempt. Harden the worker environment or worktree setup as needed.\n\nNON-SCOPE\nDo not weaken git-config-health hooks. Do not hide the failure by suppressing `git status` errors. Do not rewrite execute-bead history or skip worker isolation; the fix must preserve isolated worktree execution while protecting the primary checkout.","id":"ddx-42a4819d","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:git","area:execution","kind:bug","operator-ux","dirty-tree"],"priority":0,"schema_version":1,"status":"closed","title":"execute-bead: prevent worker attempts from mutating primary git config","updated_at":"2026-05-15T11:57:09.098109835Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:148:{"acceptance":"1. `TestInitProject_DoesNotInstallPlugins` verifies that `ddx init` with no flag creates `\u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/` and the manifest but does not write `.ddx/plugins/ddx`, `.agents/skills/ddx`, `.claude/skills/ddx`, or any project-local `plugins/` tree.\n2. `TestInitProject_LeavesPluginsForLazyResolution` verifies the initialized project can lazy-resolve the default `ddx` plugin from the global layer through `registry.ResolvePlugin` when the sibling resolver behavior is present.\n3. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/... -run 'TestInitProject_DoesNotInstallPlugins|TestInitProject_LeavesPluginsForLazyResolution'` passes.\n4. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/...` passes.\n5. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","closing_commit_sha":"3b82c8ae41c24296760ec4c7b1bd41c7bb964c8b","created_at":"2026-06-02T03:19:04.472608585Z","description":"PROBLEM\nPlain `ddx init` eagerly installs the default `ddx` plugin into every project. The intended model is that project init only creates `\u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/` and the project manifest; plugins should lazy-resolve from the global layer on first use.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- cli/cmd/init.go:73 `runInit` currently routes plain init through eager plugin setup.\n- cli/cmd/init.go:271-284 calls `registry.InstallPackageFromFS(pkg, defaultplugin.FS(), workingDir)` unconditionally, producing `.ddx/plugins/ddx`, `.agents/skills/ddx`, and `.claude/skills/ddx` in the project.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n1. Remove the default plugin install from the no-flag `ddx init` path.\n2. Keep project-local initialization of `\u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/` and the manifest intact.\n3. Ensure the no-flag path does not create or modify any project `plugins/` tree or agent-tier skill links.\n4. Add `TestPreClaimIntakeHookDispatchesOutsideProjectRoot` proving the readiness lifecycle call does not send the project root as the agent `WorkDir` and uses the intended read-only/no-write lifecycle boundary.\n2. Add `TestPreClaimDecomposerDispatchesOutsideProjectRoot` proving `runPreClaimDecomposer` dispatches outside the project root while still returning validated child proposals from JSON output.\n3. Add `TestPostLadderDecomposerDispatchesOutsideProjectRoot` proving `runDecomposer` dispatches outside the project root and only DDx store code creates children/updates the parent after validated output.\n4. Add a dirty-guard regression test (for example `TestLifecycleDispatchRejectsProjectRootMutation`) where a lifecycle dispatch attempts to write a sentinel file in the project root; the hook must not leave the project root dirty and must return a non-success lifecycle result.\n5. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'TestPreClaimIntakeHookDispatchesOutsideProjectRoot|TestPreClaimDecomposerDispatchesOutsideProjectRoot|TestPostLadderDecomposerDispatchesOutsideProjectRoot|TestLifecycleDispatchRejectsProjectRootMutation' -count=1` passes.\n6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","closing_commit_sha":"ae0a814b99ff1b458ada4934b9d242803cb44600","created_at":"2026-05-15T14:50:07.97268056Z","description":"PROBLEM\nHidden lifecycle model calls in `ddx work --watch` can mutate the operator's project checkout before a bead attempt starts. Observed on 2026-05-15 in `/Users/erik/Projects/fizeau`: readiness/decomposition for `fizeau-930b498b` dispatched `MODE: intake` / `MODE: preclaim-decompose` with the request workdir set to the Fizeau project root, and the main checkout was left dirty with `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, `internal/routehealth/probe_store.go`, `website/benchmark_responsive_test.go`, and a newly-created release-blocker bead (`fizeau-b5b6dff2`). Lifecycle calls are supposed to classify, rewrite, or propose children; they must not have a writable project-root execution context.\n\nROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE\n`cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:381-395` builds an `AgentRunRuntime` with `WorkDir: projectRoot` and dispatches the readiness model call through the normal service path.\n`cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:60-70` does the same for pre-claim decomposition, and `cli/internal/agent/recovery_decompose.go:150-161` does the same for post-ladder decomposition.\n`cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:172-176` turns an empty runtime workdir into the project workdir, and `cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:244-252` sends that workdir to Fizeau as `ServiceExecuteRequest.WorkDir`. With normal project permissions, Codex/Claude/Gemini lifecycle calls can edit files in the checkout even though DDx only needs their JSON response.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nRun all hidden lifecycle model calls (pre-claim intake, pre-claim decomposition, and post-ladder decomposition) in a DDx-owned scratch execution directory or otherwise enforce a read-only/no-write dispatch boundary. Keep reading bead/project context through DDx code, parse the model JSON response, and apply any tracker mutations through DDx store APIs after validation. Add a post-dispatch guard that fails the lifecycle call if the project root becomes dirty from anything other than DDx-owned tracker mutations performed by the orchestrator itself.\n\nNON-SCOPE\nDo not change normal `ddx try` / execute-bead worktree execution. Do not let lifecycle agents commit code or edit project files. Do not weaken decomposition output validation or tracker API usage.","events_attachment":"ddx-492050f3/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-492050f3","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:execution","area:agent","area:work","kind:bug","dirty-tree","principle:isolation"],"priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-f4aa254d","status":"closed","title":"work: isolate lifecycle model calls from project root","updated_at":"2026-05-15T16:36:24.296964643Z"}

This child owns part 1 of 3 of that parent scope.\n\nROOT CAUSE\nPROBLEM\nDDx-managed filesystem state accumulates indefinitely. On the Fizeau project (sibling repo, /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau) on 2026-05-14:\n- /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/ contained 9,735 inodes across ~9700+ directories. Many were .execute-bead-wt-* worktrees dating back to 2026-05-07 (a week stale). Each is a full repo checkout, multiplying repo size on disk.\n- .ddx/workers/ held 35 agent-loop-\u003cnano-id\u003e/ directories. Most show last_activity_at older than 1 hour with no live process. They keep persisting per-attempt status.json entries.\n- .ddx/executions/ held 44 attempt directories. Some have unmerged result.json state (a previous merge or worker crash left them in DU). No automatic GC.\n- .ddx/attachments/ holds 355 per-bead artifact directories. They flood `git status` as untracked since nothing gitignores them.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- `ddx try` creates a worktree under `/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-\u003cbead\u003e-\u003ctimestamp\u003e-\u003chash\u003e/` and only sometimes cleans up. Failure paths (worktree_lost, execution_failed, process crash) consistently leave the worktree behind. There's no startup reaper for stale worktrees.\n- `ddx work` and `ddx try` write a worker status.json under .ddx/workers/agent-loop-\u003cnano-id\u003e/ and the writer is responsible for cleanup. Process crashes leave the directory behind; no startup reaper claims them.\n- .ddx/executions/\u003ctimestamp\u003e/ is meant to be retained as evidence (per CONTRACT-003 / FEAT-010 evidence pile). But there's no retention policy: directories accumulate forever. Some land in unmerged git state after partial commits.\n- .ddx/attachments/ and .ddx/run-state*.json are written by every ddx invocation, but the project's .gitignore policy for them is inconsistent (.ddx/executions is ignored; .ddx/attachments and .ddx/run-state.json appear in `git status` constantly).\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n1. Add a startup reaper to `ddx work` and `ddx try`: on launch, scan /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/, identify worktrees whose parent ddx-try/ddx-work process is dead (no PID match), `git worktree remove` + `rm -rf` them. Bound the scan to N=200 per launch so a cold start doesn't take forever. Configurable via DDX_WORKTREE_REAP_MAX_AGE (default 72h).\n2. Add a startup reaper for `.ddx/workers/agent-loop-*/`: read status.json's `pid` and `last_activity_at`; if pid is gone OR activity \u003e 30 min stale, remove the directory.\n3. Add a configurable execution-evidence retention policy:\n...[truncated]\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nImplement only this child acceptance slice:\n- TestWorkStartup_ReapsStaleWorktrees asserts that with N pre-existing /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-* worktrees whose owning processes are dead and whose mtime is older than DDX_WORKTREE_REAP_MAX_AGE, `ddx work --once` (cold start) removes them via git worktree remove + rm -rf before claiming any bead.\n- TestWorkStartup_PreservesLiveWorktrees asserts that worktrees whose owning ddx-try/ddx-work process is still alive are NOT reaped.\n\nNON-SCOPE\nDo not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.","events_attachment":"ddx-49737227/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-49737227","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:agent","area:execution","kind:cleanup","decomposed"],"owner":"erik","parent":"ddx-c191e91a","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-179843a5","status":"closed","title":"housekeeping: reap orphan ddx worktrees and stale worker/execution state: part 1","updated_at":"2026-05-15T06:35:44.613080731Z","work-heartbeat-at":"2026-05-15T06:09:55.99701302Z"}

The same test confirms a loop.operator_attention event AND a durable operator_attention bead event (Summary \"preclaim_warn_repeated\") carry fingerprint, count (== DefaultPreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold), and distinct_bead_ids.\n3. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... green\n4. lefthook run pre-commit passes","closing_commit_sha":"835a4985f35f19afb186ad31e65652b839b209c7","created_at":"2026-05-28T09:08:21.287033509Z","description":"PROBLEM\nWhen pre_claim_intake produces a warn-only rejection with the same fingerprint repeatedly (for example a readiness schema mismatch), the worker logs a warning and goes idle every cycle (pre_claim_intake.warn -\u003e loop.idle). With many ready beads and one daemon, none get claimed and the worker looks alive while making zero progress, leaving the queue idle indefinitely.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- warn-only policy mode masks the failure: the loop emits pre_claim_intake.warn at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1859, :1982, :2009, :2034, :2073, each carrying a fingerprint computed in cli/internal/agent/readiness_decision.go:23-24, and then goes loop.idle (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1637-1649) with no guard counting consecutive identical fingerprints across distinct bead IDs to break the silent loop.\n- The threshold knob exists at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:76-79 (ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime.PreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold; zero means DefaultPreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold) and the escalation message format at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1141.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nAfter N (default DefaultPreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold) consecutive pre-claim warn outcomes sharing the same fingerprint across distinct bead IDs, emit a loop.operator_attention event and a durable operator_attention bead event (reason \"preclaim_warn_repeated\", carrying fingerprint, count, distinct_bead_ids, example_detail, example_payload), stop before claiming the threshold bead, and leave the remaining ready queue untouched. The threshold is configurable via ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime.PreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold; zero falls back to the default.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Do NOT implement the CI schema drift gate (owned by the sibling child bead).\n- Do NOT change verdict decoding or readiness payload schema.\n- Do NOT introduce go.mod replace directives.\n\nDEPENDENCIES: no deps.","events_attachment":"ddx-4c03beca/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-4c03beca","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:execute-bead","area:pre-claim","area:workers","kind:reliability","observability","priority:reliability","severity:high","decomposed","needs-human-decomposition"],"parent":"ddx-fd02cf6e","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-e2e162cd","status":"closed","title":"Escalate repeated identical pre-claim warn fingerprints to operator attention instead of looping silently","updated_at":"2026-05-28T09:29:01.582677084Z","work-no-changes-count":1}

.ddx/beads.jsonl:164:{"acceptance":"1. TestInitGlobal_CreatesAgentTierLinks asserts ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx point into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx/.\n2. TestInitProject_DoesNotInstallPlugins asserts `ddx init` (in-project) does not write to plugins/ — plugins lazy-resolve from global.\n3. A Test* in cli/cmd/doctor_test.go asserts doctor renders both the \"Global Install (...)\" and \"Project Install (...)\" lines with the correct ok|missing|lazy-resolves-to-global statuses (doctor_test.go:273,276).\n4. A test asserts the ddx-validate pre-commit hook runs skill validation only when skill files are staged, independent of install location.\n5. The SKILL.md \"How this skill works\" install-locations note is updated in this bead's commit.\n6. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/... green.\n7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"bd11f3c9adebedc32b3639a97362f4129071bab6","created_at":"2026-06-03T01:02:41.360272672Z","description":"PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)\n\nFirst-time machine setup needs an umbrella `ddx init --global` that installs the default ddx plugin globally and wires agent-tier links, while plain `ddx init` must create only the project workspace and lazy-resolve plugins (preferring project over global) rather than eagerly installing. `ddx doctor` must surface both install layers so operators can see where the skill resolves from. The ddx-validate pre-commit hook (ddx-777f4a09, already landed) must keep scoping skill checks to staged skill files regardless of install location.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- cli/cmd/init.go:75 dispatches to initGlobal (init.go:318) when --global is set; initGlobal installs the bundled default ddx plugin into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx (init.go:320), creates symlinks ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx into that dir (init.go:336), and writes ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults (init.go:351, writeGlobalConfig init.go:362).\n- Plain `ddx init` (no flag) must create \u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/ and NOT write plugins/ — plugins lazy-resolve via the registry resolver, preferring the project copy over global.\n- cli/cmd/doctor.go:421-448 computes globalPath/projectPath and the projectStatus \"lazy-resolves-to-global\" (doctor.go:436-444) and prints the \"Global Install (...) — \u003cstatus\u003e\" and \"Project Install (...) — \u003cstatus\u003e\" lines (doctor.go:447-448).\n- Before this, `ddx init` bundled plugin install with manifest creation, there was no global setup command, and doctor reported only a single install layer.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n1. `ddx init --global` installs the default ddx plugin globally, creates ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx links into ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx, and writes the global config with convention defaults.\n2. `ddx init` (no flag) creates \u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/ and does NOT install plugins; first use lazy-resolves from the project layer, falling back to global.\n3. `ddx doctor` renders two lines: \"Global Install (${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx) — ok|missing\" and \"Project Install (\u003cprojectRoot\u003e/plugins/ddx) — ok|missing|lazy-resolves-to-global\".\n4. The ddx-validate pre-commit hook continues to run skill-related validation only when skill files are staged, regardless of whether the install is global or project-scoped.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Does NOT remove the per-project install path (both layers coexist).\n- Does NOT migrate legacy ~/.config/ddx paths.\n- Does NOT add agent-harness skill paths beyond Claude Code and Codex.\n- Plugin content writing for non-init installs is owned by the install child beads.\n\nDEPS\nddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — global setup routes through ddxroot.GlobalDir(), project init through ddxroot.Path(). Governing epic: ddx-602ae31a (Principle 2). Related: ddx-777f4a09 (ddx-validate hook, landed).","events_attachment":"ddx-4c58ea3e/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-4c58ea3e","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:cli","area:config","area:plugin","kind:feature","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-04beccf3","status":"closed","title":"init/doctor: --global umbrella setup, lazy project init, and two-layer doctor reporting","updated_at":"2026-06-03T02:46:46.787576605Z"}

This child owns part 2 of 2 of that parent scope.\n\nROOT CAUSE\nPROBLEM\nParent bead ddx-f9ddaa68 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.\n\nROOT CAUSE\nPROBLEM\nddx work --watch's pre-claim intake decoder fails to unmarshal the readiness_checks response when verdict is a JSON bool. Every claim attempt produces the same warn-only rejection, so the worker logs a warning and skips. With 23 ready beads in the fizeau queue and one fizeau-scoped daemon, the entire queue is idle indefinitely.\n\nOBSERVATION (Fizeau, 2026-05-18 05:27-05:39 UTC; about an hour after the previous wedge bug ddx-8f2e0ebf)\n- Worker: PID 1527216, ddx work --watch in /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau, etime 01:22:40 at observation.\n- Log: /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau/.ddx/agent-logs/agent-loop-1779077845655338391.jsonl\n- Pattern every ~50s: resource.preflight (status ok) -\u003e pre_claim_intake.start -\u003e pre_claim_intake.warn -\u003e loop.idle.\n- Sample pre_claim_intake.warn payload:\n  {\n    \"bead_id\":\"fizeau-5b2a8950\",\n    \"decision\":\"warn\",\n    \"decision_source\":\"pre_claim_intake\",\n    \"detail\":\"decode result: json: cannot unmarshal bool into Go struct field .readiness_checks.verdict of type string\",\n    \"fingerprint\":\"15155a1cd44e96daf53eabb14c8eb3ccd8b0e5271d881208cfcbbf2869899f4f\",\n    \"outcome\":\"intake_error\",\n    \"policy_mode\":\"warn-only\",\n    \"reason\":\"system_unready\",\n    \"rule_id\":\"pre_claim_intake.system_unready\",\n    \"suggested_action\":\"check the readiness route or harness configuration and retry\",\n    \"system_reason\":\"unavailable\"\n  }\n- Identical fingerprint across multiple bead_ids confirms it is a schema mismatch, not a bead-specific issue.\n- Queue state at observation: open=44, ready=23, dependency-waiting=19. None of the 23 ready beads got claimed in the prior 30 minutes; commit log empty for the same window.\n- Other beads (fizeau-a15106ab, fizeau-90b85c8b) WERE successfully closed earlier in the night through some other path (perhaps a different worker version or a different execute-bead invocation). Suggests this decoder bug appeared mid-session OR only affects certain readiness routes.\n\nWHY THIS IS A BUG\n1. Schema drift between the readiness-check producer and the pre_claim_intake consumer. One emits verdict as bool, the other parses it as string. Either the producer or consumer changed without coordination.\n2. warn-only policy_mode masks the failure: the worker keeps polling and looks alive (loop.idle every 5\n...[truncated]\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nImplement only this child acceptance slice:\n- cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./... passes.\n- lefthook run pre-commit passes.\n\nNON-SCOPE\nDo not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.","events_attachment":"ddx-4db1875e/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-4db1875e","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:execute-bead","area:pre-claim","area:workers","kind:bug","kind:reliability","observability","priority:reliability","severity:high","ac-quality:needs-refinement","decomposed"],"parent":"ddx-61f6c57d","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-4f683421","status":"closed","title":"pre_claim_intake decoder rejects readiness_checks.verdict=bool; entire f: part 2","updated_at":"2026-05-27T09:59:20.581845803Z"}

.ddx/beads.jsonl:175:{"acceptance":"See bead body — 5 verifiable AC items: new ddxroot regression test, ~20 test fixtures updated to write config.yaml, go test -short ./internal/... green, orphan-warning at startup, pre-commit clean.","created_at":"2026-06-01T21:09:49.582933254Z","description":"**The bug**\n\n`cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go:Path()` resolves a project's state root in\none of two locations based on whether `\u003cprojectRoot\u003e/.ddx/` exists as a\ndirectory:\n\n```go\nfunc Path(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string) string {\n    inTree := InTree(projectRoot)\n    if info, err := os.Stat(inTree); err == nil \u0026\u0026 info.IsDir() {\n        return inTree    // \u003cprojectRoot\u003e/.ddx/\n    }\n    root := filepath.Join(projectsRoot(), projectIdentity(ctx, projectRoot))\n    _ = bootstrapConventionRoot(ctx, projectRoot, root)\n    return root          // ~/.local/share/ddx/projects/\u003cidentity\u003e\n}\n```\n\nThe `.ddx/` directory test is too weak. It accepts ANY directory named\n`.ddx/` as evidence that `projectRoot` is a DDx project. The home\ndirectory `$HOME` happens to have a `~/.ddx/` directory because that's\nwhere user-global state (`installed.yaml`, `plugins/`) lives via the\nparallel `JoinHome()` helper. So if any `ddx ...` invocation is made\nwith `projectRoot=$HOME` (e.g., via `cwd=$HOME` and no `--project`\noverride), `Path()` returns `~/.ddx/` as if the user's home were a\nproject root.\n\nResult: the same logical state files appear at two different paths\ndepending on how the binary was invoked, and the duplicates drift.\nThe concrete instance that triggered this investigation was the TLS\ncert: `ddx server` running with `WorkingDir=/home/erik/Projects/ddx`\n(via systemd) wrote its cert at\n`/home/erik/Projects/ddx/.ddx/server/tls/cert.pem`, while a separate\ninvocation (probably manual, from `$HOME`) wrote\n`/home/erik/.ddx/server/tls/cert.pem` — different fingerprint, same\nfilename, same purpose. The server served the project-root cert; some\nother client trusted the home-dir cert; every connection got\n`tls: bad certificate`.\n\n**Audit — every consumer of the dual-path resolver**\n\n`ddxroot.JoinProject` / `ddxroot.Path` is called from ~50 callsites\nacross the codebase. Every one of them inherits the bifurcation. The\nhighest-risk consumers (those that read or write state that must NOT\ndiverge between locations):\n\n- `beads.jsonl` (`execute_bead.go:2165`, `execute_bead.go:2722`,\n  `execute_bead_dangling_success.go:189`, `agentmetrics/loader.go:244`,\n  `service_run.go:396`, `server.go:797`, `server.go:5043`, `exec/store.go:56`)\n- `metrics/locks.jsonl` (`lockmetrics/lockmetrics.go:96`)\n- `metrics/attempts.jsonl` (`attemptmetrics/attemptmetrics.go:99`)\n- `.git-tracker.lock` (`lockmetrics/lockcap.go:221`)\n- `server/tls/cert.pem` + `key.pem` (`server/server.go:326`)\n- `tsnet/` (`server/server.go:503`)\n- `workers/\u003cid\u003e/` (`execution_cleanup.go:1370`, `workerstatus/liveness.go:44`,\n  `executions_mirror.go:211`)\n- `executions/\u003cattempt-id\u003e/` (`prose_evidence.go:125`,\n  `reviewer_stream_artifact.go:41,45`, `agentmetrics/loader.go:23,27`)\n- `agent-logs/` (`execution_cleanup.go:1268`)\n- `config.yaml` (`init.go:155`, `execution_cleanup.go:221`,\n  `execution_worktree_root.go:23`)\n- `exec/definitions/`, `exec/runs/` (`exec/path_helpers.go:14,18`)\n- `graphs/` (`docgraph.go:1197`)\n- `run-state.json` + `RunStateDir` (`agent/run_state.go:60,64`)\n- `tracker_lock.go:116` (the bead tracker lock)\n\nAnything in this list reachable from two different `projectRoot`\narguments (or with one of them being `$HOME`) ends up duplicated.\n\n**Proposed fix (structural)**\n\nTighten the in-tree check. A bare `.ddx/` directory is necessary but\nnot sufficient evidence of a project root. Require an initialization\nmarker: `\u003cprojectRoot\u003e/.ddx/config.yaml` (the file written by\n`ddx init` at `cli/cmd/init.go:236`). Patch:\n\n```go\nfunc Path(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string) string {\n    inTree := InTree(projectRoot)\n    if isInitializedInTree(inTree) {   // was: os.Stat / IsDir\n        return inTree\n    }\n    root := filepath.Join(projectsRoot(), projectIdentity(ctx, projectRoot))\n    _ = bootstrapConventionRoot(ctx, projectRoot, root)\n    return root\n}\n\nfunc isInitializedInTree(dir string) bool {\n    info, err := os.Stat(dir)\n    if err != nil || !info.IsDir() {\n        return false\n    }\n    _, err = os.Stat(filepath.Join(dir, \"config.yaml\"))\n    return err == nil\n}\n```\n\n`ExistingPath` gets the same treatment.\n\nThis makes `Path($HOME)` correctly fall through to\n`~/.local/share/ddx/projects/\u003cidentity\u003e` (since `~/.ddx/` has no\n`config.yaml`), eliminating the dual-location bifurcation entirely.\n\n**Blast radius (this is what blocks landing the patch as-is)**\n\nA surveyed initial spike against this patch made `internal/ddxroot`\ntests pass, but exposed ~20 test fixtures elsewhere that build a bare\n`.ddx/` directory and rely on the old \"any `.ddx/` = project root\"\ncontract. Failing packages from a quick `go test -short ./internal/...`\nrun:\n\n- `internal/server/graphql` (9 failures: `TestAgentMetrics_*`,\n  `TestNode_ResolvesBeadByID_ScopedToProject`, `TestNode_ResolvesExecRunByID_ScopedToProject`)\n\nFiles that need their test fixtures updated to also write a `config.yaml`\nin the `.ddx/`:\n\n- `internal/server/graphql/resolver_agent_metrics_test.go` (fixtureWorkingDir)\n- `internal/server/graphql/resolver_workingdir_ctx_test.go`\n- `internal/agent/execution_cleanup_test.go` (3 callsites)\n- `internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_interrupted_attempt_test.go` (2 callsites)\n- `internal/agent/execute_bead_commit_test.go`\n- `internal/agent/execute_bead_worktree_cleanup_test.go`\n- `internal/agent/serviceconfig_test.go`\n- `internal/agent/drain_regression_test.go`\n- `internal/agent/operator_prompt_backlinks_test.go`\n- `internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_cleanup_test.go` (6 callsites)\n- `internal/agent/execute_bead_artifacts_test.go`\n- `internal/agent/execution_resources_test.go` (3 callsites)\n\nA central helper (e.g., `testutils.MakeInitializedDDxRoot(t, dir)`)\nthat creates `.ddx/` + writes a stub `config.yaml` would cap the diff.\n\n**Migration**\n\nExisting user-global state at `~/.ddx/server/tls/`, `~/.ddx/tsnet/` etc.\ngets orphaned by the fix (they were created when `Path($HOME)`\nreturned `~/.ddx/`; afterwards Path uses the XDG path). The transition\nis graceful for TLS (server regenerates a fresh self-signed cert) but\noperators with manual config in those subdirs will need a one-shot\nmigration. Recommend:\n\n1. Emit a startup warning when `~/.ddx/server/` exists alongside the\n   XDG path, telling the operator to delete the orphan.\n2. Or auto-move `~/.ddx/server/` → `~/.local/share/ddx/projects/\u003chost-id\u003e/server/`\n   on first server start under the new resolver.\n\n**Operational note**\n\nThe original sindri-dev TLS bad-cert storm (~36 hours, blocking the\nentire ddx work queue) was unblocked by manually copying the home-dir\ncert+key over the project-root location and restarting ddx-server.\nThat's a band-aid; this bead is the proper unwind.\n\n**Severity: P1**\n\nThe bug is latent across most state files. The reason it manifested as\n\"server unreachable\" rather than \"bead tracker corrupted\" is that\nnobody was running ddx commands as `$HOME` against beads files\nrecently. Any operator who does (or any future feature that uses\n`projectRoot=$HOME` for any reason) will silently bifurcate state.\n\n**Acceptance**\n\n1. `go test ./internal/ddxroot/...` includes a new regression test\n   (`TestDDxRoot_BareInTreeFallsThroughToXDG`) that verifies a `.ddx/`\n   without `config.yaml` falls through to the XDG path.\n2. All 20+ test fixtures listed above updated to write\n   `.ddx/config.yaml` (or use the new testutils helper).\n3. `go test -short ./internal/...` passes.\n4. Startup or migration logic warns about orphan `~/.ddx/server/` /\n   `~/.ddx/tsnet/` directories.\n5. TestWorkLoop_IdlePassEpicClosureIsIdempotent: a second idle pass closes zero additional epics and does not error.\n3. TestWorkLoop_EpicWithOpenChildNotClosedByIdlePass: an epic with one open child is not closed.\n4. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'TestWorkLoop_.*Epic' green.\n5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"fe5c06c06bfce323eceb5fedd714053ddbcad7cb","created_at":"2026-06-05T22:06:45.072804498Z","description":"PROBLEM: FEAT-004 §Queue Semantics For Epics (docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md:134-136,158-161) requires the ddx work idle-path remediator to fire a \"closure cascade\" auto-action that closes epics whose children are all terminal — and forbids the loop from skipping/exiting while ready epics remain. Today the loop only REPORTS EpicClosureCandidates (internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:3432,3461; cmd/work.go:345) and SKIPS them as epic_container (execute_bead_loop.go:3694-3695). The recovery hook (internal/agent/recovery_hook.go:71-94) dispatches reframe/decompose/park but has NO closure action. So an all-children-closed epic is reported and skipped, never closed — a FEAT-010 reliability-contract violation.\n\nROOT CAUSE: no idle-path step invokes the closure path; recovery_hook.go lacks a closure-cascade remediation.\n\nPROPOSED FIX: Add an idle-path remediation step (before declaring \"no execution-eligible beads\") that calls Store.EpicClosureCandidates(ctx) (store.go:1702) and closes each via Store.Close (which already emits the epic_auto_close audit event and runs the walk-up, including the cancelled-terminal + all-cancelled-cancels refinement from the CloseWithEvidence bead). Idempotent: closed epics drop out of EpicClosureCandidates so a second pass closes nothing new. Record closures in the work-log snapshot (reuse EpicClosureCandidatesCount, execute_bead_loop.go:3461).\n\nNON-SCOPE: the CloseWithEvidence hook (separate bead); reconcile; recursion.","events_attachment":"ddx-514f36d1/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-514f36d1","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","kind:bug","kind:reliability","phase:iterate","spec:FEAT-004","epic-autoclose"],"priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-03e0a3c2","status":"closed","title":"epic-autoclose: add idle-path closure-cascade remediation to ddx work","updated_at":"2026-06-06T07:16:29.80231001Z"}

Single commit, subject `chore(deps): bump fizeau v0.14.0 → v0.14.1 [ddx-\u003cid\u003e]`.\n6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","claimed-at":"2026-05-15T01:10:40Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"2755295","closing_commit_sha":"05bacc221ae63af30204bff1ce39e39473d7487c","created_at":"2026-05-15T01:08:26.888393189Z","description":"PROBLEM\nDDx pins github.com/easel/fizeau v0.14.0. Fizeau just shipped v0.14.1 with three routing-reliability fixes that together resolve the queue-stall problems we hit all session:\n\n1. Bounded TCP dial timeout (5 s) for openai-compat clients — closes the gap that v0.14.0 left open (the IsDialError short-circuit was wired but the dial itself was still ~30s). This addresses fizeau-b5757624.\n\n2. Policy-aware scoring under MinPower/MaxPower bounds — `--min-power 8` now correctly prefers cheaper in-bounds subscription candidates over metered higher-power candidates. Without this, ladder-aware routing in ddx-8a7a6843 (landed) does not consistently pick the right provider when both are eligible.\n\n3. Proactive endpoint aliveness probing + ServiceOptions.PersistRouteHealth — startup TCP-connect probe and background re-probe loop record per-provider reachability. The routing gate filters known-unreachable providers with FilterReasonEndpointUnreachable instead of dialing them every request. PersistRouteHealth (optional, path-based) persists probe results across ddx process boundaries, so a fresh ddx-try does not re-discover that bragi is dead. This addresses fizeau-7cd66e6b.\n\nUntil this bump lands, autorouting on this machine still pays ~5s per dead-provider dial on every new ddx-try, the cheap subscription is not always picked when in-power-bounds, and provider health is rediscovered fresh on each invocation. With the bump, the routing chain is end-to-end correct.\n\nROOT CAUSE\ncli/go.mod line: `github.com/easel/fizeau v0.14.0`. Needs to be v0.14.1.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- `go get github.com/easel/fizeau@v0.14.1` from cli/, regenerate go.sum.\n- `go build ./...` green.\n- `go test ./internal/agent/... -run \"Cooldown|Retry|Routing|Provider|Land|Lock\" -count=1` green (smoke).\n- Single commit, `chore(deps): bump fizeau v0.14.0 → v0.14.1`.\n- DOES NOT wire ServiceOptions.PersistRouteHealth — that requires the DDxRoot epic (ddx-602ae31a) to settle on a path. Follow-up bead can wire it once DDxRoot lands. The bump itself unblocks the dial-timeout and policy-scoring fixes immediately; PersistRouteHealth defaults to off in v0.14.1 so the bump is safe without DDx-side wiring.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Wiring PersistRouteHealth.\n- Any DDx-side routing/escalation changes.\n- Changes to provider configuration.\n\nDEPS\nNone. This is a pure dependency bump.\n\nTOP PRIORITY because every running drain on this machine pays the v0.14.0 dial-timeout cost on every dead-provider attempt, and the in-flight epic (ddx-602ae31a) child beads will reference the new behaviour as the baseline.","events_attachment":"ddx-51e9076c/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-51e9076c","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:deps","area:routing","kind:bug","phase:2","spec:endpoint-first-routing"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-31abc515","status":"closed","title":"deps: bump fizeau v0.14.0 → v0.14.1 — dial timeout, policy-aware scoring, endpoint probing","updated_at":"2026-05-15T01:12:59.170515599Z","work-heartbeat-at":"2026-05-15T01:10:40.681758599Z"}

TestPluginLookup_FallsBackToGlobal (cli/internal/registry/resolve_test.go): with the project copy absent and only the global copy present, `ResolvePlugin` returns layer==\"global\" and the path returned by `GlobalPluginDir(name)`.\n3. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/registry/... green.\n4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"ecb015c2fb2effc5bff40afd42cf058b2606c65e","created_at":"2026-06-03T07:17:39.015088022Z","description":"PROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)\nRuntime plugin lookup must follow a single authoritative precedence — install-in-project (project tier) overrides install-to-agent (global tier), with the embedded default ddx plugin as the last-resort baked-in fallback. Without one resolver, install/update/doctor each reimplement path logic and drift, so a project-local plugin can be silently shadowed by a global copy (or vice-versa).\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- cli/internal/registry/resolve.go:29 `ResolvePlugin(ctx, projectRoot, name)` is the intended single entry point. It checks the project path `ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot)/plugins/\u003cname\u003e` (resolve.go:30), then the global path `ddxroot.GlobalDir()/plugins/\u003cname\u003e` (resolve.go:35), then returns layer `\"baked-in\"` with an empty path only for name==\"ddx\" (resolve.go:41), else errors (resolve.go:44).\n- cli/internal/registry/resolve.go:15 `GlobalPluginDir(name)` defines the global-tier path `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/\u003cname\u003e`.\n- The precedence contract is currently under-tested, so regressions in ordering would go undetected.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n- Guarantee `ResolvePlugin` returns layer `\"project\"` and the project path when the plugin dir exists in BOTH the project and global tiers (project wins).\n- Guarantee it returns layer `\"global\"` and the global path when the project copy is absent but the global copy exists.\n- Keep baked-in (empty path, caller uses `defaultplugin.FS()`) valid only for name==\"ddx\"; return an error for an unknown plugin present in no tier.\n- Add table-driven coverage in cli/internal/registry/resolve_test.go using `t.Setenv(\"XDG_DATA_HOME\", ...)` and a temp projectRoot with an in-tree `.ddx/` so both tiers are exercised deterministically.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Changing how install/update/doctor WRITE plugins (covered by sibling beads).\n- Removing the existing per-project install (both modes coexist).\n- Migrating any legacy ~/.config/ddx paths.\n- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.\n\nDEPS\n- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution): `ResolvePlugin` routes through `ddxroot.Path()` and `ddxroot.GlobalDir()`; both must be agreed-on. No other deps — this bead is the foundation the install/update/doctor siblings build on.","events_attachment":"ddx-536c3dd2/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-536c3dd2","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:plugin","area:config","kind:feature","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-b67715d8","status":"closed","title":"registry: ResolvePlugin honors project→global→baked-in precedence with table tests","updated_at":"2026-06-03T07:21:01.247260548Z","work-no-changes-count":1}

.ddx/beads.jsonl:199:{"acceptance":"1. The bead tracker reader, when it encounters a parse failure, emits an error whose cited line number is either (a) the real on-disk line number of the failing record, computed via byte-offset → newline scan, or (b) explicitly tagged as a partial-read diagnostic (e.g. \"partial record at byte offset N; concurrent writer suspected\"). Verified by a unit test that constructs a JSONL with a known corrupted line and asserts the error cites that real line number.\n2. TestBeadReader_ConcurrentWriteDoesNotEmitFalseLineNumber — spawn a writer that appends a long record byte-by-byte to a temp JSONL file; concurrently run a reader scan; the reader either retries cleanly OR emits an error whose message does NOT cite a line number that is fully written and parseable in the final file. Tagged as `t.Parallel` and run with `-race -count=10` to catch the race.\n3. TestBeadReader_GenuineCorruptionStillSurfaces — tracker file with a deliberately corrupted record (truncated mid-JSON, garbage bytes) produces a louder error than partial-read transients, and the error cites the real file line.\n4. Existing bead-tracker tests continue to pass without modification.\n5. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/bead/... -count=1 -race green.\n6. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./... green.\n7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"rev-ddx-5efb1616","created_at":"2026-05-17T01:31:46.298121627Z","description":"PROBLEM\nddx work's readiness check emits a misleading parse error during routine bead reads, blaming a specific line number that is NOT actually corrupt. Observed in /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau today at 19:25:58 during pickup of fizeau-8ebac59d:\n\n  19:25:58 readiness check: starting\n  bead: read line 18: bead: unmarshal: unexpected end of JSON input\n  23:26:03 readiness route claude/opus reason=policy=default; score=99.5\n\nThe route still succeeded — the error is non-fatal — but the operator-facing log line tells them line 18 of .ddx/beads.jsonl is corrupt when it is not. python3 json.loads on every line of the 210-line file returns no errors; line 18 specifically is a 10441-byte fully-valid JSON record.\n\nROOT CAUSE (hypothesis — verify in fix)\nThe \"line 18\" in the error message is almost certainly NOT a line in the on-disk JSONL file. Plausible causes:\n1. A relative buffer offset inside a streaming json.Decoder that has been mistaken for a file line number when formatting the error.\n2. A partial read race: the tracker is being read concurrently with a write (auto-doc-stamp from `ddx bead create`/`ddx bead update`), and the reader hit EOF mid-record. The \"line 18\" is the line index inside the partial buffer, not the file.\n3. A bead's events array contains an embedded string with newlines that the reader's line counter is tokenizing on, throwing off the line index.\n\nThe error is non-fatal because the reader presumably skips the bad bead and continues — but it surfaces to the operator as if the file itself is corrupt, prompting them to inspect / fsck / restore a perfectly good tracker file.\n\nREPRO\nObservable any time `ddx work` (and likely `ddx bead list`, `ddx work plan`, etc.) reads the tracker while another `ddx bead create`/`update`/`dep add` is mid-write. Most reliably reproducible on a project with active auto-doc-stamp commits flying (i.e., any project running `ddx work` continuously). The 210-line fizeau beads.jsonl reproduces it under contention.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n1. **Fix the error formatting.** The error should report either:\n   - The actual on-disk line number where the parse failed (compute via byte offset / newline scan).\n   - OR \"(partial record at byte offset N)\" if a streaming decoder hit EOF mid-record.\n   - Never report a number that is not a real file line, especially when telling operators to \"reconcile manually.\"\n2. **Distinguish \"transient partial read\" from \"persistent corruption.\"** A partial read during concurrent write should be retried (with a short backoff or via a read lock around the tracker file). A genuine corruption should fail the read and surface a louder error. Today both look identical to the operator.\n3. **Add coverage.** Test that simulates concurrent write+read on a tracker file (write a long record byte-by-byte while the reader does a full scan) and asserts the reader either retries cleanly OR reports an accurate diagnostic. Failure: today's behavior of \"line 18: unmarshal: unexpected end of JSON input\" against a non-line-18 problem.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Replacing JSONL with a different tracker format.\n- Adding a file lock around all tracker writes (probably needed eventually but out of scope here — this bead is strictly about diagnostics + transient-read handling).\n- The actual readiness-check race condition that caused the read in the first place — that's incidental; any concurrent reader would hit it.\n\nPARENT AND DEPS\nNo parent. No deps.\n\nEVIDENCE FILE\nWorker log preserved at /tmp/fizeau-worker-1778973834.log (line containing \"bead: read line 18: bead: unmarshal: unexpected end of JSON input\"). 'ddx bead create --depends-on X --parent Y' with X == Y (or X an ancestor of Y) HARD-REJECTS at creation time with the same error shape as AC #1. No silent rewrite.\n3. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./... passes.\n4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","closing_commit_sha":"2bbb93e87a3920ca453101fdeaea9565f382c185","created_at":"2026-06-06T03:22:30.475161508Z","description":"PROBLEM\nParent bead ddx-e19185aa is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 3 of that parent scope.\n\nROOT CAUSE\nThe decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:\n\n  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --\u003e child_A\n  e29892a4 -- parent --\u003e child_B\n  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           \u003c- BAD: parent is also a dep\n  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  \u003c- BAD\n\nThe parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.\n\nObserved scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):\n- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')\n- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)\n- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]\n- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores\n- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order\n\nRoot cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.\n\nThere is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nImplement only this child acceptance slice:\n- 'ddx bead dep add \u003cbead\u003e \u003cdep\u003e' HARD-REJECTS the edge when \u003cdep\u003e is \u003cbead\u003e's parent OR any ancestor in the parent chain. Error message names the offending ancestor and prints the relevant parent-chain. Test: ddx_bead_dep_add_rejects_parent_ancestor.\n- 'ddx bead create --depends-on X --parent Y' with X == Y (or X an ancestor of Y) HARD-REJECTS at creation time with the same error shape as AC #1. No silent rewrite.\n\nNON-SCOPE\nDo not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.","events_attachment":"ddx-63780ef6/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-63780ef6","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:tracker","area:lifecycle","area:decomposition","kind:safety","kind:correctness","ac-quality:needs-refinement","decomposed"],"parent":"ddx-e19185aa","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-7e9e7432","status":"closed","title":"Reject bead dep edges that re-introduce the parent or any ancestor: part 1","updated_at":"2026-06-07T08:20:25.284955863Z","work-no-changes-count":4}

The intended model is: `ddx init --global` does one-time machine setup (global default plugin + agent-tier links + global config), while plain `ddx init` only creates `\u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/` and lazily resolves plugins from the global layer on first use.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- cli/cmd/init.go:73 `runInit` always installs the default ddx plugin (init.go:271-284 via `registry.InstallPackageFromFS(pkg, defaultplugin.FS(), workingDir)`), producing `.ddx/plugins/ddx`, `.agents/skills/ddx`, `.claude/skills/ddx` unconditionally.\n- There is no `--global` flag and no `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml` writer.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n1. Add `--global` bool flag to `ddx init` (cli/cmd/init.go).\n2. init --global: install the default `ddx` plugin to `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx` (via the install-global path from the sibling install bead), create agent-tier links `~/.claude/skills/ddx` and `~/.agents/skills/ddx` pointing into the global plugin dir, and write `${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml` with convention defaults.\n3. init (no flag): create `\u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/` and the manifest, but do NOT install any plugin — plugins lazy-resolve via `registry.ResolvePlugin` (sibling resolver bead), preferring project over global, on first use. The governing plan at docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-05-13-ddx-skill-package-layout.md:16-23 requires the default `ddx` plugin package to own the DDx skill, requires `ddx init` and `ddx plugin install ddx` to share one package installer path, and keeps home-directory skill installs out of scope.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n  Current state is split across several bootstrap paths: cli/cmd/init.go:256-287 copies embedded skills with `skills.Install(skills.SkillFiles, ...)`, registers `.ddx/skills/ddx` separately, then installs the default plugin through `registry.InstallPackage`; cli/internal/registry/installer.go:110-132 handles package skills by calling `skills.Install` directly instead of applying manifest skill mappings; cli/cmd/install.go:254-372 has a separate local symlink implementation; cli/Makefile:65-84 keeps copied skill mirrors in sync. The plan requires these paths to collapse into package install semantics.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n  Break the migration into child beads that first make package install semantics correct, then route init through the embedded package installer, then move the skill source into the default package layout, and finally update docs/specs/help.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n  - Do not execute the child implementation work in this epic bead.\n  - Do not restore global or home-directory skill installs.\n  - Do not change public plugin CLI names beyond the separate CLI rationalization work.\n\nPARENT + DEPS\n  Root epic. No deps.","id":"ddx-6862f2ac","issue_type":"epic","labels":["phase:2","area:skills","area:plugins","kind:epic","plan-2026-05-13","spec:plan-2026-05-13-ddx-skill-package-layout"],"priority":1,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"plan-2026-05-13-ddx-skill-package-layout","status":"open","title":"skills package: migrate DDx skill ownership to the default plugin package","updated_at":"2026-05-13T16:18:19.688755898Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:220:{"acceptance":"`ddx install --force --local \u003cpath\u003e` against a repo containing broken symlinks ONLY under gitignored paths completes successfully. The validation logic either consults .gitignore or skips well-known scratch/dotfile paths.","closing_commit_sha":"08558b9bffef71ec63fdf2485ee4f4d054d8c166","created_at":"2026-05-18T02:12:47.205231666Z","description":"## Summary\n\nObserved against ddx v0.6.2-alpha103-12-ge6379ebc5 on 2026-05-18.\n\n`ddx install \u003cplugin\u003e --force --local \u003crepo\u003e` validates ALL symlinks in the source tree, including those under gitignored paths. Result: any scratch/tmp file with a stale symlink (e.g. files written by a previous `ddx doctor` invocation under doctor/home/.codex/tmp/) breaks the install despite being gitignored and never intended to ship.\n\n## Reproduction\n\n1. In a repo with `doctor/home/.codex/tmp/` gitignored (matches `.codex`)\n2. Run any operation that creates broken symlinks under that dir (e.g. an old codex CLI install no longer present)\n3. Run `ddx install helix --force --local /path/to/helix`\n4. Install fails: `validating package structure: ... broken symlink target ...`\n\nRepro on HELIX:\n```\nddx install helix --force --local /home/erik/Projects/helix\n# Error: validating package structure:\n#   doctor/home/.codex/tmp/arg0/codex-arg0zt0PZe/apply_patch: broken symlink target ...\n```\n\nWorkaround: rm -rf the offending tmp paths and retry.\n\n## Expected behavior\n\n`ddx install --local` should skip files that are gitignored in the source repository (run via `git check-ignore -v \u003cpath\u003e` or read `.gitignore` directly). At minimum, it should skip paths the user could not have intended to ship: well-known scratch/tmp directory names, dotfiles outside the plugin manifest's declared paths, etc.\n\n## Related\n\n- Related to (but distinct from) ddx-efadca32 (lifecycle worktree empty) filed 2026-05-16.\n- Blocks HELIX's `ddx install helix --force` for any operator with tmp scratch state present.","events_attachment":"ddx-686564bf/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-686564bf","issue_type":"bug","labels":["kind:bug","area:install","area:validation","observed-failure"],"owner":"easel","priority":1,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-eec2ad7d","status":"closed","title":"ddx install --local walks gitignored paths and fails validation on broken symlinks in scratch/tmp dirs","updated_at":"2026-05-27T03:18:37.806916401Z"}

Today the global install and update paths must write to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/ and link into the home-tier agent skill dirs, using a separate global state file so they never touch a project's install.\n\nROOT CAUSE\n- `ddx install` carries the `--global` flag at cli/cmd/install.go:49 and routes through `resolveInstallDirs(global)` (install.go:184) and `selectInstallState(global)` (install.go:202). For global=true, resolveInstallDirs returns installRoot=`ddxroot.GlobalDir()`, agentSkillsDir=`~/.agents/skills`, claudeSkillsDir=`~/.claude/skills` (install.go:185-193); selectInstallState returns `registry.LoadGlobalState`/`SaveGlobalState` (install.go:206-208). Targets are remapped by `adjustInstallTargets`/`remapSkillTargets` (install.go:215,235). Global installs skip the project auto-commit (install.go:174).\n- `ddx update --global` routes through `performGlobalUpdate` (cli/cmd/update.go:300), which operates exclusively on `registry.LoadGlobalState()`/`ddxroot.GlobalDir()` (update.go:301,337,345) and never touches the project tree; the flag is parsed at update.go:395 and dispatched at update.go:66.\n- The `--global` flag must also be present on `ddx plugin list`/`ddx plugin show` so query surfaces can target the global tier.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n1. Verify `ddx install \u003cname\u003e --global --silent` writes the plugin under ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/ and creates a symlink-or-copy under ~/.claude/skills/\u003cname\u003e and ~/.agents/skills/\u003cname\u003e, recording the result in the global state file (not the project state).\n2. Verify `ddx update --global` updates only the global plugin tree and leaves any project install untouched.\n3. Add/confirm `--global` on `ddx plugin list` and `ddx plugin show`.\n4. Document the global install location in library/skills/ddx/SKILL.md \"How this skill works\".\n5. The ddx-validate pre-commit hook (ddx-777f4a09) must continue to scope skill validation to staged skill files regardless of install location.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Runtime lookup precedence (sibling child — ResolvePlugin).\n- Project (non-global) install behavior and init/doctor wiring (sibling children).\n- Removing the existing per-project install entirely (both modes coexist).\n- Migrating any ~/.config/ddx/ legacy paths.\n- Agent-harness path discovery beyond Claude Code and Codex.\n\nDEPS\n- ddx-06cbaa90 (ddx home resolution) — global install routes through `ddxroot.GlobalDir()`.","events_attachment":"ddx-6931d485/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-6931d485","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:cli","area:plugin","kind:feature","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas","triage:no-changes-unjustified"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-93a1e107","status":"closed","title":"install/update: --global surface installs to XDG global tree + agent-tier skill links","updated_at":"2026-06-03T17:16:46.067151221Z","work-no-changes-count":1}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:228:{"created_at":"2026-05-21T03:25:29.25797088Z","description":"PROBLEM\nThe docker-clone attempt backend is unusable on a remote, rootless, or de-nested Docker daemon (e.g. OrbStack on this machine, docker info Name=orbstack). Every attempt fast-fails in 17-39ms before any agent work with: docker: Error response from daemon: invalid mount config for type \"bind\": bind source path does not exist: \u003chost path\u003e. Outcome is classified execution_failed / transport.\n\nROOT CAUSE\ncli/internal/agent/attempt_backend.go:612 dockerRunArgs() constructs the container with five classes of HOST bind-mount (--mount type=bind,src=\u003chostpath\u003e):\n  - :622 src=ws.WorkDir            dst=/work               (cloned repo)\n  - :623 src=exe                   dst=/usr/local/bin/ddx  (ddx binary, os.Executable())\n  - :636 src=ws.DockerRun          dst=/ddx-runtime        (HOME/cache/tmp + injected creds)\n  - :641-642 gocache, work-tmp                              (Go build/test cache)\n  - :644-649 dockerToolMounts() -\u003e claude/codex/gemini      (agent CLIs via exec.LookPath)\nA de-nested/remote daemon's filesystem namespace does not include this machine's /home/erik/... , so every src path 'does not exist' from the daemon's view. Confirmed: 4 attempts on ddx-b5816493 failed this way; failing paths included /home/erik/.local/bin/ddx and /home/erik/.local/share/claude/versions/2.1.145. A short -v probe 'succeeded' only because docker silently auto-created an empty dir (the classic -v footgun), proving the source was not visible.\n\nPROPOSED FIX (decomposed into child tasks)\nAdopt the standard create -\u003e cp -\u003e start -\u003e cp-out -\u003e rm pattern used for remote/rootless daemons, plus bake static tooling into the image and use daemon-managed named volumes for caches. Only the daemon's own storage (image layers + named volumes) is reliably reachable; everything dynamic is copied in/out.\n  1. Bake agent CLIs into the attempt-runner image; drop their bind-mounts.\n  2. Keystone: move workspace + ddx binary in via docker create/cp instead of bind.\n  3. Convert Go caches + runtime dir to named volumes.\n  4. Inject credentials via env/docker cp instead of the runtime-home bind.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- The worktree and local-clone backends (unaffected; worktree is the working default).\n- Routing/provider selection.\n\nNOTE: filed from an operator session after switching the live queue drain to --attempt-backend worktree to work around this. Child tasks carry their own file:line scope and AC.","id":"ddx-6967f6e6","issue_type":"epic","labels":["area:agent","kind:bug","reliability","spec:FEAT-010"],"priority":2,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"FEAT-010","status":"open","title":"docker-clone attempt backend: eliminate host bind-mounts for remote/de-nested daemon compatibility","updated_at":"2026-05-21T03:25:29.25797088Z"}

This child owns part 5 of 5 of that parent scope.\n\nROOT CAUSE\nPROBLEM (Principle 2 of epic ddx-602ae31a)\n\nSkills, personas, and plugin content need to install in two distinct locations depending on operator intent:\n\n- **install-to-agent (global)**: written under the user's agent-tier paths (e.g. ~/.claude/skills/\u003cname\u003e, ~/.codex/skills/\u003cname\u003e, or the equivalent each harness reads). Skills and personas are then available across every project on the machine without per-project setup. In in-tree mode that is \u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/; in convention mode that is ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/\u003chost\u003e/\u003cowner\u003e/\u003crepo\u003e/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/. Either way the project-scoped agent paths point into it (e.g. \u003cproject\u003e/.claude/skills/ link target).\n\nToday `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, and `ddx init` all assume per-project installs only. Add `--global` flag to `ddx install`, `ddx update`, `ddx plugin list`, `ddx plugin show`. When set:\n   - Resolve target: ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/.\n   - Write the plugin content there.\n   - Create symlinks (or copies, OS-dependent) into the agent-tier skill paths the user's agents read: ~/.claude/skills/\u003cname\u003e, ~/.codex/skills/\u003cname\u003e, and any others the agent registry knows about.\n   - Skill discovery from a fresh shell finds the skill via the agent-tier path.\n\n2. Without `--global`, `ddx install` writes to ddxroot.Path()/plugins/\u003cname\u003e/ and links into \u003cproject\u003e/.claude/skills/\u003cname\u003e AND \u003cproject\u003e/.agents/skills/\u003cname\u003e. In convention mode (no \u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/) the project's agent-tier paths still point into the XDG project dir's plugins. In in-tree mode they point into \u003cproject\u003e/.ddx/plugins/.\n\n3. `ddx init --global` is\n...[truncated]\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nImplement only this child acceptance slice:\n- cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./cmd/... ./internal/registry/... green.\n- lefthook run pre-commit passes.\n- Spec updates (ADR/skill-topology + CLAUDE.md Persona System) landed in this bead's commit.\n\nNON-SCOPE\nDo not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.","events_attachment":"ddx-6a23cbd3/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-6a23cbd3","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:config","area:plugin","area:cli","kind:feature","phase:3","initiative:zero-footprint","principle:skills-personas","decomposed"],"parent":"ddx-937de9fc","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-46eb6782","status":"closed","title":"skills/personas/plugins: install-to-agent vs install-in-project — and: part 5","updated_at":"2026-06-03T23:00:54.031349686Z"}
